title: Field Marketing Manager
description:
This role is based in New York City.

The Role:

The Field Marketing Manager is the bridge between MFUSED’s brand strategy and its in\-market execution. This role leads regional field marketing efforts, ensuring that the brand is represented consistently and with excellence across retail locations, events, and activations.

As a hands\-on leader and role model, the Field Marketing Manager sets the tone for their team through action — never asking the team to do work they are not willing to do themselves. This person drives engagement through example, taking full ownership of their team’s success and fostering a culture where everyone is “all in to win it.”

They are responsible for coaching and developing field teams, strengthening retail and budtender relationships, supporting consumer education, and gathering real\-time market insights that shape how MFUSED shows up in every region. This role requires agility, leadership, strong communication skills, and a balance of strategy and execution.

**What You'll Do**

Field Team Leadership \& Development

* Serve as a role model for professionalism, work ethic, and collaboration — setting the standard for excellence in the field.
* Lead, train, and coach Field Marketing Coordinators to execute high\-quality activations, ensuring consistent brand representation and compliance.
* Take ownership of team performance and results, understanding that success is a collective effort achieved through accountability and teamwork.
* Participate directly in field activities as needed to support business priorities — leading by example and fostering a hands\-on, “all\-in” culture.
* Evaluate team performance regularly, providing real\-time feedback, mentorship, and development opportunities to strengthen capability and confidence.

Retail \& Budtender Engagement

* Build and maintain strong relationships with dispensary partners, store managers, and retail staff to maximize MFUSED’s retail performance and visibility.
* Coordinate and deliver budtender training sessions to educate retail teams on MFUSED products, brand values, and our unique value proposition.
* Ensure retail displays, point\-of\-sale (POS) materials, and activations uphold brand standards and compliance requirements.
* Gather insights from retail teams on product performance, consumer preferences, and competitor activity to inform HQ strategy.

Event \& Activation Oversight

* Partner with the Event Marketing Manager to plan and execute regional events, trade shows, and consumer activations.
* Oversee team participation, setup, and teardown to ensure each event delivers an engaging, on\-brand experience.
* Manage logistics and scheduling to ensure activations are properly staffed and executed on time.

Consumer \& Market Insights

* Gather consumer feedback and insights through field interactions and retailer relationships.
* Assess MFUSED’s retail displays and merchandising performance relative to competitors and share observations with HQ.
* Translate field and consumer insights into actionable recommendations for brand, product, and retail improvements.

Cross\-Functional Collaboration

* Collaborate with Brand, Product Marketing, and Channel Marketing teams to ensure local activations reflect broader campaign goals.
* Partner with Sales and Operations to align in\-market activity with product availability, lifecycle updates, and business priorities.
* Work with Marketing Operations and Compliance to ensure all field activities meet regulatory standards.

Performance Management \& Reporting

* Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) for field activations, retail performance, and consumer engagement.
* Track and manage regional budgets, expenses, and inventory for marketing materials.
* Provide detailed reports to the Senior Manager, Field Marketing \& Training on regional execution, team performance, and opportunities for improvement.

Other Duties as Assigned

* Perform additional duties or special projects as assigned to meet business needs, including supporting retail launches, company initiatives, or cross\-departmental marketing efforts.

What You Bring

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field (or equivalent combination of education and experience).
* 5\+ years of experience in field marketing, retail marketing, or brand activation, preferably within cannabis, CPG, or lifestyle industries.
* Demonstrated success leading regional or distributed teams with a focus on accountability, motivation, and mentorship.
* Proven ability to lead by example — maintaining a strong “all\-in” attitude and willingness to step in wherever needed.
* Experience training retail staff or budtenders on product knowledge and brand storytelling.
* Strong understanding of retail operations, merchandising, and consumer engagement strategies.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills — able to speak confidently with retailers, budtenders, consumers, and internal teams.
* Highly organized and adaptable, with exceptional problem\-solving and time management skills.
* Analytical skills with experience capturing and interpreting field data and consumer insights.
* Reliable transportation, valid driver’s license, and ability to travel between retail locations, team meetings, and events as required.
* Willingness to travel frequently within assigned markets and work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends for events.
* Hands\-on leadership style grounded in humility, accountability, and collaboration.

Physical Demands / Work Environment

This position requires frequent travel within assigned regions and regular presence at retail locations, events, and activations. The role involves standing for extended periods, driving between sites, and occasional lifting of up to 25 pounds. Reasonable accommodations will be made in accordance with applicable law.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement:

MFUSED is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.

Why Join MFUSED

At MFUSED, we believe great leaders lead from the front. As a Field Marketing Manager, you’ll inspire your team by being in the trenches with them — training, executing, and celebrating success together. You’ll drive a culture of accountability and pride, ensuring that every activation, retail interaction, and training reflects the excellence of our brand. If you’re a leader who believes that success is a shared journey — and that being “all in to win it” starts with leading by example — this is your opportunity to make an impact on one of the most innovative brands in cannabis.

Internal applicants: If you’re currently a MFUSED team member and interested in this opportunity, please apply through the internal career portal or contact HR directly for guidance. Internal candidates will be given full and fair consideration consistent with MFUSED’s policies and values.

At MFUSED, we're excited to review your application and welcome you to our mission of excellence and inclusivity in the Cannabis world.

Pay: $45,000\.00 \- $65,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Application Question(s):

* What compensation are you are looking for in a new position?

Experience:

* Management: 1 year (Preferred)

Ability to Commute:

* New York, NY (Required)

Willingness to travel:

* 75% (Preferred)

Work Location: On the road

title: Field Marketing Manager
description:
**About Monjur, Inc.**


Monjur is the Contract Intelligence platform built exclusively for Managed Service Providers (MSPs).

  

For more than two decades, our team of MSP attorneys has helped service providers structure, negotiate, and enforce the contracts that define their customer relationships.

  

Today, that experience powers Monjur Pilot — our AI legal assistant — which helps MSP teams answer contract questions, redline agreements, and manage negotiations without waiting for outside counsel.

  

Unlike generic AI tools that rely on internet data, Pilot is grounded in Monjur’s proprietary legal knowledge base and supervised by licensed MSP attorneys. The result is a system that gives MSPs the speed of AI while maintaining the legal oversight required for real contractual decisions.

  

Instead of static templates or one\-time legal engagements, Monjur provides an ongoing legal system that evolves with our clients’ businesses.

  

Our platform includes:

* MSP\-specific contract standards
* Attorney\-supervised legal guidance and updates
* A centralized Legal Knowledge Base
* Smart integrations with ConnectWise, Autotask, HaloPSA, Kaseya, and Microsoft
* Pilot, Monjur’s AI legal assistant

  

Today, more than 950 MSPs rely on Monjur to standardize their agreements, reduce legal uncertainty, and move faster during sales and negotiations.

  

Monjur exists for one reason:

  

To help MSP leaders sleep better at night knowing their legal foundation is taken care of.

  

We are a growing team of attorneys, operators, and technologists building the legal infrastructure that MSPs rely on to scale with confidence.

  

If you are excited about combining AI, legal expertise, and industry\-specific software to solve real operational problems, you will feel at home at Monjur.

### **The Role**


We’re hiring a **Field Marketing Manager** to own the planning, coordination, and execution of Monjur’s field and event marketing program. This is not a strategy\-in\-theory role, or a "figure out if events work for us" role. We’ve already built events into a meaningful growth channel. We've completed 100\+ shows over the last two to three years.


Trade show leads close at \~50% with an \~11\-day sales cycle. The product\-market fit is proven.

  

What we need now is an operator — someone who can run a high\-volume event program with precision, improve process and follow\-through, and ensure every event delivers measurable business impact.

  

You’ll report to the Director of Marketing and partner closely with Sales, RevOps, leadership, and external vendors. You’ll be responsible for making sure events run cleanly from end to end: logistics are locked down, sales is fully prepared, leads are captured and routed quickly, and performance is clearly reported.

### **What You’ll Do**

* Own Monjur’s field and event marketing program from planning through post\-event reporting
* Manage event execution across trade shows, regional events, partner events, dinners, and community gatherings
* Build and maintain repeatable systems for pre\-event planning, internal coordination, lead capture, follow\-up, and retrospective review
* Partner with Sales to align on target accounts, on\-site expectations, event goals, and follow\-up ownership
* Coordinate all event logistics, including booth setup, shipping, materials, swag, travel, vendor communication, and on\-site readiness
* Ensure lead capture tools and processes are configured, tested, and working before each event
* Partner with RevOps to ensure leads are tagged, routed, enriched, and entered into HubSpot quickly and accurately
* Drive post\-event reporting on pipeline created and influenced, conversion rates, and cost per opportunity
* Identify which events should be expanded, improved, cut, or added based on performance data
* Coordinate side events and relationship\-building opportunities that deepen partnerships in the MSP ecosystem
* Collaborate with the broader marketing team on event\-related content capture, pre\-event outreach, post\-event follow\-up, and co\-marketing support

### **What Success Looks Like**

**In your first 30 days:**

* You’ve taken full ownership of the event program
* You understand the event calendar, vendor relationships, logistics, and internal handoffs
* You’ve documented the current state, identified gaps, and established a planning cadence with Marketing and Sales

**By 90 days:**

* Events are running through a repeatable, reliable process
* Sales is prepared at least two weeks before each show
* Leads are captured and entered into HubSpot within 24 hours
* Post\-event retrospectives are completed within one week
* Field marketing and sales are operating with a clear feedback loop

**By 6 months:**

* Every event has clear performance reporting
* Pipeline created and influenced, conversion rates, and cost per opportunity are tracked consistently
* Leadership receives post\-event reports within two weeks of each show
* You’ve made data\-backed recommendations on which events to keep, cut, or add

### **What You’ll Work With**

**Cross\-functional partners**

* Director of Marketing
* Sales team
* RevOps
* CEO and COO
* Content/social team
* External creative agency

**Tools and systems**

* HubSpot
* Hapily
* Event platforms and lead capture tools
* Existing vendor and sponsorship relationships

**Types of events**

* Major MSP conferences
* Regional events and peer groups
* Monjur\-hosted dinners and community events
* Partner and co\-marketing events

### **What We’re Looking For**

* Experience owning field marketing, event marketing, or experiential marketing programs end to end
* Strong project management and operational discipline in fast\-moving environments
* Proven ability to manage multiple events and timelines simultaneously without details slipping
* Experience coordinating closely with sales teams before and after events
* Familiarity with CRM workflows, lead routing, campaign tracking, and event attribution
* Comfort working inside tools like HubSpot and event engagement platforms
* Strong vendor management and logistics coordination skills
* Ability to balance hands\-on execution with process improvement and performance analysis
* Confidence operating in a lean team with high accountability and high visibility
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills

### **Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in B2B SaaS, MSP, channel, or community\-driven go\-to\-market environments
* Experience with event attribution and reporting in partnership with RevOps
* Experience supporting co\-marketing or partner\-driven events
* Familiarity with Hapily or similar event engagement platforms

### **What Makes This Role Different**


This is a chance to step into a program that already works and make it materially better. You won’t be proving the value of events from scratch — you’ll be improving an engine that already produces results. You’ll have direct access to leadership, visible ownership of a major growth channel, and the opportunity to build structure around a program with real momentum.

### **What to Know Before You Apply**

* Travel is significant and likely 40–50%, depending on the season
* The pace is fast and events often cluster around major conference windows
* Data quality and attribution are improving, but not yet perfect
* The team is lean, so this role requires hands\-on execution
* Priorities can shift quickly, and success requires steadiness, adaptability, and strong judgment

### **Why Join Monjur**


If you’re energized by live events, thrive in operationally intense environments, and want to own a program that directly impacts pipeline and revenue, this role offers real responsibility and visible impact. You’ll help turn a strong event engine into a disciplined growth machine.

 **Why Monjur**

* You will help define and scale Attorney\-Supervised Contract Intelligence.
* You will work alongside experienced legal and operational leaders.
* Your work will have direct, measurable impact on how MSPs manage risk and scale.

**Monjur operates at the intersection of legal precision and operational scale. We are building long\-term infrastructure for a category that demands both.**

 **If you believe you can contribute meaningfully to Monjur’s mission, even if you do not meet every qualification listed, we encourage you to apply.**

 **The pay range for this role is:**  

85,000 \- 125,000 USD per year(United States)

title: Field Marketing Manager
description:
**Company Overview:**


Cellebrite’s (Nasdaq: CLBT) mission is to enable its global customers to protect and save lives by enhancing digital investigations and intelligence gathering to accelerate justice in communities around the world. Cellebrite’s AI\-powered Digital Investigation Platform enables customers to lawfully access, collect, analyze and share digital evidence in legally sanctioned investigations while preserving data privacy. Thousands of public safety organizations, intelligence agencies and businesses rely on Cellebrite’s digital forensic and investigative solutions—available via cloud, on\-premises and hybrid deployments—to close cases faster and safeguard communities.


To learn more, visit us at www.cellebrite.com, https://investors.cellebrite.com/investors and find us on social media @Cellebrite.  



  

**Position Overview:**


This is not a booth\-and\-banner job. You will be the marketing team’s operator in the field, embedded with sales, running programs that generate qualified pipeline for Cellebrite’s Digital Investigation Platform across law enforcement, defense, and enterprise accounts in the United States.


You will plan and execute regional campaigns, events, and ABM plays that produce measurable pipeline within 30 days. You will work side by side with account executives, SDRs, and sales leadership to identify target accounts, build programs that move deals forward, and report on results with the same rigor our customers apply to evidence.


Our customers are federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies, defense and intelligence organizations, and enterprise legal and compliance teams. They handle sensitive material in criminal investigations and national security. You need to understand their world well enough to show up with relevance, not just marketing collateral.

 **Pipeline Generation and Acceleration:**

* Build and execute regional campaigns that drive new logo and expansion pipeline across your territory.
* Partner with AEs, SDRs, and ISRs to align on target accounts, territory plans, and follow\-up expectations.
* Design workshops, executive briefings, roundtables, and technical deep dives that move deals from early stage to commit.
* Identify stuck deals with your AEs and build targeted acceleration plays to get them moving.
* Track sourced and influenced pipeline. Optimize programs based on conversion rates and cost per opportunity.

 **Event Strategy and Execution:**

* Own end\-to\-end event planning: venue selection, logistics, promotion, staffing, content, and budget.
* Build and execute pre\-event, on\-site, and post\-event workflows to maximize qualified meetings and lead capture.
* Ensure timely lead routing, follow\-up sequences, and event ROI reporting.
* Represent Cellebrite at industry events, conferences, and trade shows (IACP, HTCIA, IACIS, regional user forums, and similar).

 **Regional GTM and Localization:**

* Tailor corporate messaging, assets, and offers to regional market realities and customer needs.
* Develop regional playbooks and competitive insights that support territory strategy.
* Capture on\-the\-ground feedback and route it to product marketing, demand gen, and content teams.

 **Sales Alignment:**

* Act as the day\-to\-day marketing partner for your region’s sales team. Attend QBRs, pipeline reviews, and forecast calls.
* Train sales reps to use campaigns, content, and event assets effectively.
* Coordinate ABM programs with sales and marketing ops, including target account lists, engagement tactics, and account penetration tracking.

 **Reporting and Budget:**

* Monitor field performance metrics: sourced pipeline, influenced pipeline, ACV, velocity, and cost per acquisition.
* Manage your regional field marketing budget. Allocate spend based on ROI and pipeline needs.
* Maintain dashboards for sales and marketing leadership with clear, honest reporting.

  

Requirements:
  

* 5\+ years in field marketing, demand generation, or event\-driven pipeline roles in B2B technology.
* Direct experience partnering with enterprise sales teams and a track record of marketing\-sourced pipeline you can quantify.
* Comfort operating in government, law enforcement, defense, or regulated enterprise environments. You understand procurement cycles, grant funding, and the difference between a chief and a commissioner.
* Strong project management instincts. You can run five events in parallel without dropping details.
* Proficiency with marketing automation (Marketo, HubSpot, or similar), CRM (Salesforce), and event platforms.
* Analytical mindset. You build reports because you want to know what worked, not because someone asked.
* Willingness to travel 40\-50% within your region. Some weeks that means three cities. Other weeks it means your home office.

 **Preferred:**

* Experience marketing to law enforcement, public safety, or justice agencies.
* Familiarity with digital forensics, eDiscovery, or investigative technology.
* Background in channel or partner marketing alongside direct field programs.
* Experience with ABM platforms (Demandbase, 6sense, or similar).

  

Office Location:
  

Remote

title: Field Marketing Representative
description:
**About** **Stronghouse**



Strong on the Inside \& Out. We know nothing's more valuable than what takes place on the inside of a home, the family moments that strengthen bonds. The lived experiences that shape communities. title: Artist Relations | Campaign Manager
description:
**Artist Relations \& Campaign Manager (Pathway Role)**

**Company:** Eighti Republic

**Location:** Remote (Atlanta\-based preferred)

**Reports To:** Vice President of Operations

**About Eighti Republic**

Eighti Republic is a hybrid production company and record label focused on building and monetizing music intellectual property across artist development, sync, and distribution. We operate within a structured ecosystem that connects production, marketing, and release strategy to move projects from creation to execution.

We are not a traditional label or management company. We are building a system designed for consistent output, scalable campaigns, and long\-term artist growth.

**Position Overview**

We are seeking an **Artist Relations \& Campaign Manager** to oversee artist\-facing operations and manage the execution of marketing campaigns across releases.

This role is responsible for ensuring that artists are properly supported, campaigns are structured and executed effectively, and all moving parts—from content to rollout—are aligned and delivered on time.

This is a **performance\-based pathway role** designed for individuals who want to grow into a long\-term position within artist development, label operations, and campaign strategy.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Artist Relations**

* Serve as the primary point of coordination between artists and internal teams
* Ensure artists are prepared for releases, content creation, and campaign execution
* Maintain consistent communication and alignment with artists throughout project cycles

**Campaign Management**

* Plan and oversee single release campaigns from preparation to rollout
* Ensure all required digital assets (content, visuals, social media materials) are completed
* Coordinate timelines and execution across production, marketing, and creative teams

**Marketing Execution Oversight**

* Direct and monitor intern activity across:
* social media growth strategies
* influencer outreach
* fan page development
* TikTok and short\-form content strategies
* Ensure all campaign deliverables are executed properly and on schedule

**Operations \& Coordination**

* Track project timelines and campaign progress
* Identify bottlenecks and ensure all releases move forward without delays
* Report artist progress and campaign performance to leadership

**Growth \& Opportunity Identification**

* Identify opportunities to increase artist visibility and engagement
* Assist in developing long\-term strategies for artist growth and positioning

**Qualifications**

* Experience in artist relations, marketing, or creative project coordination
* Strong organizational and time management skills
* Understanding of digital marketing, social media strategy, and campaign rollout
* Excellent communication and ability to manage relationships
* Ability to operate independently and handle multiple projects
* Interest in artist development and long\-term growth within the music industry

**Preferred Experience**

* Experience working with artists, labels, or creative teams
* Familiarity with release strategy, streaming platforms, and digital campaigns
* Experience managing or directing others (interns, creatives, or teams)

**Compensation**

This is a **build\-stage, performance\-based role**.

* No base salary initially
* Compensation is tied to **campaign performance, releases, and artist\-related initiatives**
* Opportunity to earn through execution, results, and contribution to the system

**Growth Path**

* Structured pathway into a **salaried Artist Relations / Label Operations role**
* Target transition within **3–6 months**, based on performance
* Long\-term opportunity to grow into leadership within artist development and campaign strategy

**Evaluation Period**

All candidates will begin with a **4\-week evaluation period (20 hours/week)** focused on:

* campaign execution
* communication and organization
* ability to manage artists and marketing initiatives

**Additional Requirements**

* Atlanta\-based preferred (for occasional in\-person sessions and events)
* Willingness to travel when needed

Job Types: Part\-time, Internship

Pay: $12\.00 \- $18\.00 per hour

Application Question(s):

* “What specific results have you produced for an artist or brand (streams, followers, engagement, campaign performance)? Please include numbers.”
* “If you were responsible for releasing a single next week, what are the top 3 actions you would take immediately to drive attention?”
* “This role is performance\-based with no base salary initially. Are you comfortable working in that structure? If yes, briefly explain why.”

Work Location: Remote

title: Integrated Marketing Director
description:
We anticipate the application window for this opening will close on \- 4 May 2026
At MiniMed, you can begin a lifelong career of exploration and innovation, while helping make a difference in the lives of people living with diabetes around the globe. You'll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation for a more connected, compassionate world.


**About the Role**
==================

The Director of Integrated Marketing, Americas is the regional leader responsible for translating global marketing strategy into localized, demand\-driving programs that fuel commercial growth across the Americas. While global platforms and brand direction are established by the Global Marketing organization, this role serves as the critical bridge ensuring those strategies are effectively deployed, localized, and optimized across HCP, install base, distributor, and launch audiences. This role owns end\-to\-end product launch and sustaining strategies for current and future product platforms — shaping commercial messaging, program architecture, and field\-ready tools that ultimately help more people access life\-changing diabetes technology.
**Key Responsibilities**

Go\-to\-Market Strategy \& Program Architecture


* Lead Regional Demand Programs: Develop and own end\-to\-end marketing programs spanning HCP, install base, growth, distributor, and product launch audiences across the Americas.
* Own Campaign Architecture: Create and maintain the Americas program and campaign framework, ensuring strategic alignment with global direction while reflecting regional market nuances.
* Drive Product Launches: Lead the launch of new pump, pen, integrated CGM, and future products for the Americas, from strategy through field execution.
* Engage Ambassadors, Spokespeople \& Advocacy Partners: Develop and steward a regional network of patient ambassadors, brand spokespeople, and advocacy organizations that authentically represent MiniMed's mission and extend reach across key communities in the Americas.

Content, Messaging \& Field Enablement


* Localize Global Strategy: Own the content localization strategy, ensuring global messaging is adapted to meet the needs and expectations of Americas customers and stakeholders.
* Enable the Sales Organization: Develop and maintain marketing messages, programs, and support tools that empower field teams to drive utilization and achieve forecast.
* Represent the Americas Voice: Serve as the primary Americas voice\-of\-customer within the global marketing organization, influencing global strategy with regional insights.
* Engage HCPs \& KOLs: Build and activate a regional HCP engagement strategy and KOL network that drives clinical credibility, supports product adoption, and ensures the latest evidence is embedded into field\-ready messaging and education programs.
* Congress \& Convention Strategy: Contribute to MiniMed's presence at key Americas diabetes congresses and conventions, partnering with Events team, Medical Affairs and KOLs to align content with commercial priorities and extend HCP engagement across the full event lifecycle.

Performance, Analytics \& Lifecycle Management


* Analyze \& Act: Leverage business trends and customer insights to identify new growth opportunities and optimize in\-market programs.
* Sustain Product Lifecycle: Own sustaining product lifecycle management decisions, including growth strategies and discontinuation recommendations.
* Quarterly Planning: Develop quarterly Plans of Action in collaboration with marketing and regional partners, ensuring teams are equipped to deliver against commercial goals.

Travel up to 20%


**Must Have: Minimum Requirements:**  

*To be considered for this role, please ensure the minimum requirements listed below are evident on your resume.*  

Bachelor’s Degree with 10\+ years of marketing, sales, or program management experience and 7\+ years of direct people leadership experience
  

OR
  

Masters Degree with 8\+ years of marketing, sales, or program management experience and 7\+ years of direct people leadership experience


**Nice to Have:**

* MBA or other Advanced Degree
* Experience managing teams through matrixed organizational structures.
* Matrix Navigation: Proven ability to drive results in a "Global/Regional" model where tools and strategy are centralized, but execution is localized.
* Industry Expertise: Experience in Medical Device or Life Sciences, particularly within the Diabetes therapeutic area.
* Commercial Acumen: Strong sales orientation and experience partnering with field\-based sales forces.

The base salary range is applicable across the United States, excluding Puerto Rico and specific locations in California. The offered rate complies with federal and local regulations and may vary based on factors such as experience, certification/education, market conditions, and location. Compensation and benefits information pertains solely to candidates hired within the United States (local market compensation and benefits will apply for others).


**Physical Job Requirements**
=============================

The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this position, but they are not an exhaustive list of all the required responsibilities and skills of this position.


The physical demands described within the Responsibilities section of this job description are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. For Office Roles: While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to be independently mobile. The employee is also required to interact with a computer and communicate with peers and co\-workers. Contact your manager or local HR to understand the Work Conditions and Physical requirements that may be specific to each role.


**Benefits \& Compensation**
============================

**MiniMed offers a competitive salary and flexible benefits package**

At MiniMed, we put people first. A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values: We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every stage of your career and life.


Salary ranges for U.S (excl. PR) locations (USD):$184,800\-$277,200  

  

At MiniMed, we are committed to supporting the well\-being and financial security of our employees. Regular employees working 20 or more hours per week are eligible for a robust benefits package, including health, dental, and vision insurance, as well as access to a Health Savings Account, Healthcare Flexible Spending Account, life insurance, long\-term disability leave, and a dependent daycare spending account. In addition, all regular employees enjoy incentive plans, a 401(k) plan with company match, short\-term disability coverage, paid time off and holidays, participation in our Employee Stock Purchase Plan, and access to our Employee Assistance Program. Eligible employees may also benefit from our Non\-qualified Retirement Plan Supplement and Capital Accumulation Plan, subject to IRS minimum earnings requirements. Please note that “regular employees” refers to those who are not temporary staff, such as interns, and some benefits may not apply to employees in Puerto Rico.


For further details about our comprehensive benefits, we encourage you to visit the link below.


MiniMed Benefits Overview


**About MiniMed**
=================

MiniMed is a full\-stack insulin delivery company dedicated to supporting people living with diabetes through every step of their journey — when and how they need it. For more than 40 years, we’ve been committed to redefining what’s possible: intelligent dosing systems designed for real life, predictive insights that stay a step ahead, and always on support when it’s needed most. At the heart of everything we do is a simple Mission: to make every day a better day for people with diabetes.  



Learn more about our business, and our mission here .

title: Campaign Manager
description:
Job Description
Campaign Manager
Requisition ID: 3982
Job Location: New Holland \- Pennsylvania \- United States, Racine \- Wisconsin \- United States
Category for Job Posting: Sales and Marketing
Job Type for Job Posting: Full Time* 
**About Us**
------------

Innovation. Sustainability. Productivity. This is how we are Breaking New Ground in our mission to sustainably advance the noble work of farmers and builders everywhere. With a growing global population and increased demands on resources, our products are instrumental to feeding and sheltering the world.


From developing products that run on alternative power to productivity\-enhancing precision tech, we are delivering solutions that benefit people – and they are possible thanks to people like you. If the opportunity to build your skills as part of a collaborative, global team excites you, you’re in the right place.


**Grow a Career. Build a Future!**

Be part of this company at the forefront of agriculture and construction, that passionately innovates to drive customer efficiency and success. And we know innovation can’t happen without collaboration. So, everything we do at CNH Industrial is about reaching new heights as one team, always delivering for the good of our customers.


**Job Purpose**
---------------

The Campaign Manager is responsible for the planning, execution, and performance optimization of integrated marketing campaigns designed to drive both wholesale and retail sales through an independent dealer network to agricultural end\-users.


This role sits within the Commercial Marketing team and serves as the central orchestration point between the Business Segment Lead (market share owner), product management (Product Marketing Organization), dealer channel stakeholders, a captive finance organization, and parts and service business units.


The Campaign Manager ensures that commercial objectives translate into actionable, measurable campaigns that generate demand, enable dealers, and support profitable growth.


**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

**Campaign Strategy \& Execution**

* Translate annual and quarterly sales objectives into integrated campaign plans that drive wholesale orders and retail demand.
* Develop campaign briefs grounded in segment strategy, product positioning, competitive landscape, and seasonal demand cycles.
* Lead campaign execution across advertising agencies and external vendor partners (creative, media, digital, experiential, CRM, etc.).
* Ensure message consistency across national, regional, and dealer\-level activations.
* Oversee production timelines, budgets, and performance milestones.

**Dealer Channel Enablement**

* Design campaigns that support dealer sell\-in (wholesale) and sell\-through (retail) objectives.
* Equip dealers with marketing toolkits, co\-op guidance, promotional assets, and retail offers.
* Align campaign timing with dealer inventory levels and sales priorities.
* Partner with field teams to gather dealer feedback and optimize activation effectiveness.

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Partner with the captive lender and pricing and programming team to integrate retail finance programs, rate promotions, and leasing offers into campaign architecture.
* Coordinate with Parts \& Service organizations to incorporate lifecycle revenue strategies and after\-sales attachment opportunities.
* Collaborate with Product Managers to ensure new product launches include:
* + Clear value propositions
	+ Competitive differentiation
	+ Pricing and promotional strategy
	+ Sales enablement materials
* Support go\-to\-market planning for new product introductions including tradeshow or farm show strategies executed through the tradeshow and events team

**Performance Management \& Analytics**

* Establish marketing KPIs aligned to wholesale shipments, retail sales, market share growth, and campaign ROI.
* Monitor performance across funnel metrics (awareness, consideration, purchase, ownership and re\-purchase).
* Optimize campaigns based on real\-time performance data and market feedback.
* Provide structured reporting to Business Segment Lead and Commercial Leadership.

**Budget \& Vendor Management**

* Manage campaign budgets to maximize efficiency and return on investment.
* Evaluate agency and vendor performance against defined deliverables and KPIs.
* Ensure compliance with brand standards and commercial guidelines.
**Experience Required**
-----------------------

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field.
* 5\+ years of experience in campaign management, preferably within durable goods, heavy equipment, or dealer\-distributed industries.
* Demonstrated experience managing advertising or marketing agencies and external partners.
* Strong understanding of multi\-channel marketing (digital, media, experiential, dealer programs).
* Experience working within or alongside a distribution/dealer channel model.
* Analytical proficiency with marketing performance metrics and ROI measurement.
* Excellent cross\-functional communication and stakeholder management skills.
**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

**Pay Transparency**
--------------------

The annual salary for this role is USD $86,625\.00 \- $132,825\.00 plus any applicable bonus (Actual salaries will vary and will be based on various factors, such as skill, experience and qualification for the role.)


**What We Offer**
-----------------

We offer dynamic career opportunities across an international landscape. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to delivering value for all our employees and fostering a culture of respect.


At CNH, we understand that the best solutions come from the diverse experiences and skills of our people. Here, you will be empowered to grow your career, to follow your passion, and help build a better future. To support our employees, we offer regional comprehensive benefits, including:


* *Flexible work arrangements*
* *Savings \& Retirement benefits*
* *Tuition reimbursement*
* *Parental leave*
* *Adoption assistance*
* *Fertility \& Family building support*
* *Employee Assistance Programs*
* *Charitable contribution matching and Volunteer Time Off*
  



US applicants: CNH Industrial is an equal opportunity employer. This company considers candidates regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status. Applicants can learn more about their rights by viewing the federal "Know Your Rights" poster here . CNH Industrial participates in E\-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I\-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S. You can view additional information here .  

  



Canada applicants: CNH Industrial is an equal opportunity employer. This company considers candidates regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, nationality, place of origin, disability, marital status, family status, age, or any other ground prohibited by applicable provincial human rights legislation.

title: Senior Field Marketing Manager
description:
**Job Description**
-------------------


LifeWorks is seeking a Senior Field Marketing Manager to partner closely with the LifeWorks Marketing Director in driving and scaling strategic marketing initiatives across a diverse West Coast portfolio. This role is both strategic and hands\-on, owning scalable programs while remaining deeply embedded in the field to ensure strong execution across cafés, catering, markets, and experiential events.


Developing new skills and doing what it takes to get the job done are central to success in this role. To meet evolving business and client needs, responsibilities may change over time and new duties may be assigned as required.

**This position requires approximately 70% travel both west coast and nationally as well as an in\-person presence in the bay area five days per week to support teams, clients, and on\-site execution.**

 *LifeWorks Restaurant Group is a hospitality\-driven restaurant company built for the modern workplace. We design and operate chef\-led cafés, catering programs, and food experiences that go far beyond transactions, creating moments of connection, culture, and care in everything we do.*

*Rooted in culinary excellence and powered by creativity, LifeWorks brings together food, design, and service to build brands within brands, tailoring each experience to the people and communities we serve. Our work lives at the intersection of hospitality and strategy, elevating everyday dining, strengthening workplace culture, and turning food into a meaningful part of how organizations engage their teams.*  

  

*At LifeWorks, people make the difference. That belief shapes how we lead, how we partner, and how we bring hospitality to life across every café, every campus, and every client relationship.*

**Compensation Data**
---------------------


COMPENSATION: The salary range for this position is $95,000 to $125,000\. If both numbers are the same, that is the amount that Aramark expects to offer. This is Aramark?s good faith and reasonable estimate of the compensation for this position as of the time of posting.

  

BENEFITS: Aramark offers comprehensive benefit programs and services for eligible employees including medical, dental, vision, and work/life resources. Additional benefits may include retirement savings plans like 401(k) and paid days off such as parental leave and disability coverage. Benefits vary by location and are subject to any legal requirements or limitations, employee eligibility status, and where the employee lives and/or works. For more information about Aramark benefits, click here Aramark Careers \- Benefits \& Compensation.

  

There is no predetermined application window for this position, the position will close once a qualified candidate is selected. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable law, including, but not limited to, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the California Fair Chance Act, and the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance to the extent that those laws apply to the opportunity.

**Job Responsibilities**
------------------------

**Strategic Leadership \& Marketing Program Development**

* Translate LifeWorks brand and growth strategies into actionable, scalable marketing programs across cafés, catering, markets, and events.
* Lead and activate a Marketing Champion Network across client accounts, building training, tools, and peer communities that drive consistent execution.
* Partner cross\-functionally with culinary, operations, design, procurement, and communications to align marketing initiatives with enterprise goals.
* Develop and deliver onboarding and ongoing training for site leaders and front\-line teams focused on brand standards, merchandising, and execution excellence.
* Create and maintain toolkits, signage standards, event setup guides, and seasonal activation playbooks to support field adoption.
* Establish reporting tools to measure marketing effectiveness, champion engagement, and program impact.
* Partner with the Field Marketing Director to analyze performance data and translate insights into action.
* Develop presentations and storytelling assets that communicate results to clients and senior leadership.

**Operational Field Execution \& Client Support**

* Work directly with operators to create and implement signage, marketing collateral, and merchandising plans aligned with LifeWorks and client brand standards.
* Provide hands\-on, on\-site support for café and catering launches, special events, promotions, and seasonal activations.
* Support the field with smallwares ordering, display materials, and setup needs to ensure teams are equipped to execute marketing programs.
* Assist with product merchandising and visual presentation standards across accounts.
* Support marketing and programming for cafés, catering, and pantry programs to drive participation and revenue.
* Build strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders, presenting new ideas and activation plans aligned with business goals.
* Conduct regular site visits to audit execution, gather feedback, and identify opportunities to elevate the on\-site experience.
* Protect the integrity of both LifeWorks and client brands in all communications, materials, and activations.
**Qualifications**
------------------

* 8\-10\+ years of experience in field marketing, brand marketing, retail merchandising, or hospitality marketing, with proven success in multi\-site or client\-facing environments.
* Bachelor?s Degree in Marketing, Business, Hospitality Management, or a related field strongly preferred.
* Advanced proficiency in Canva and working knowledge of Adobe InDesign, with the ability to produce field\-ready signage, toolkits, and presentation materials.
* Experience in contract dining services, food service, retail, or multi\-unit hospitality environments strongly preferred.
* Demonstrated ability to think both creatively and strategically, balancing brand vision with operational execution.
* Proven success working in fast\-paced, matrixed environments with internal and external partners.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including client presentations, team training, and executive\-facing storytelling.
* Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build trust across operators, clients, and senior leadership.
* Solid understanding of social and digital marketing fundamentals and the ability to guide teams on best practices.
* Exceptional time management and prioritization skills, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives at once.
* Strong command of Microsoft Office, especially PowerPoint, for executive\-ready presentations.
* Collaborative, solutions\-oriented leader with a hands\-on, can\-do mindset.
* Willingness to travel approximately 70% across the West Coast and national accounts as needed.
* Ability to work in person four days per week to support field teams and client needs.
* Location: The Bay Area, West Coast.
**Education**
-------------

**About Aramark**
-----------------

**Our Mission**


Rooted in service and united by our purpose, we strive to do great things for each other, our partners, our communities, and our planet.


At Aramark, we believe that every employee should enjoy equal employment opportunity and be free to participate in all aspects of the company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender, pregnancy, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, military status, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by applicable law.


**About Aramark**


The people of Aramark proudly serve millions of guests every day through food and facilities in 15 countries around the world. Rooted in service and united by our purpose, we strive to do great things for each other, our partners, our communities, and our planet. We believe a career should develop your talents, fuel your passions, and empower your professional growth. So, no matter what you're pursuing \- a new challenge, a sense of belonging, or just a great place to work \- our focus is helping you reach your full potential. Learn more about working here at http://www.aramarkcareers.com or connect with us on Facebook, Instagram and Twitter.

title: Regional Marketing Director
description:
Overview:

Founded in 1965 by Mack Pogue as Lincoln Property Company, Willow Bridge Property Company is one of the United States’ most respected full\-service residential property companies, operating a successful portfolio in 75 markets. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, Willow Bridge proudly employs over 5,500 team members, has built over 200,000 apartments and manages 220,000 units for its clients. To learn more, visit willowbridgepc.com. **We create places people want to call home — starting with you.**  

With decades of proven success, Willow Bridge is a national leader in residential property management and development, active in 29 states and managing more than 230,000 apartment homes across the U.S.
At Willow Bridge, our purpose drives everything we do. Whether you’re launching your career or looking to take the next step, we’re here to support your growth with intention, flexibility, and real opportunities to make an impact.
We’re currently hiring for a **Regional Marketing Director** — someone who brings fresh thinking, follows through, and knows how to bring people together to get results. We value people who lead by listening, communicate with clarity, and take initiative to find better ways forward. If you're someone who believes in showing up with integrity, working collaboratively, and driving positive change, you’ll feel right at home here.  

Let’s grow together at Willow Bridge.
Responsibilities:
* Focus on targeting budgeted occupancy and revenue by providing support, guidance and direction to a portfolio of multifamily assets through the development and implementation of strategic marketing programs.
* Collaborate with the national marketing team to establish, implement and maintain the goals and objectives of Willow Bridge Property Company.
* Understand and correctly articulate search engine optimization and search engine marketing goals for individual assets to regional and on\-site team members as well as ownership groups.
* Research and build community marketing plans for existing and potential new business that attracts the identified target audience and strategically addresses business objectives, which are evaluated on an ongoing basis.
* Create and maintain marketing budgets for individual communities.
* Maintain relationships with marketing vendor partners to review portfolio performance on a monthly or quarterly basis.
* Develop and create marketing materials for potential new business and efficiently and effectively present materials and ideas at proposal meetings.
* Regularly communicate with ownership groups, asset managers, and regional executive team members regarding marketing performance of any particular asset and / or overall portfolio.
* Manage and help complete marketing assignments for community management transitions and collect all digital assets.
* Collaborate with marketing and design agencies for new construction and rebrand projects and assist with creative direction, project management, timeline oversight, creation of collateral materials, website production, photography, etc.
* Assist with new development floorplan review and pre\-development efforts as defined by the VP or EVP.
* Manage software integrations with internal and external parties to ensure dynamic pricing and availability is listed at all times on community websites and ILS’.
* Ensure data accuracy in Property Management Software systems and make updates as necessary.
* Ensure community photos and videos are of good quality, modern, optimized and published on the correct channels.
* Set\-up and continually monitor reputation management for each community and assist team members with strategies to improve while overseeing review responses.
* Implement and manage lead tracking and lead management while continually analyzing reporting and making necessary recommendations.
* Train and manage on\-site teams to utilize lead management, iPad leasing programs, and other marketing software correctly and efficiently.
* Set\-up and review Google Analytics to examine and analyze website performance on an ongoing basis.
* Develop, implement, and oversee social media content and strategies including set\-up, optimization, paid and promoted campaigns, contests, and / or contracted agency strategies.
* Setup, claim, and assist with the ongoing monitoring of major social media websites and locational websites (i.e. Google My
* Business, Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, etc.) as defined by the national marketing department.
* Collaborate with on\-site associates to evolve innovative strategies based on market conditions and property data that will directly impact leasing velocity.
* Assist stabilized and repositioned communities who are experiencing marketing challenges and poor occupancy.
* Oversee and provide training to local and regional teams to reinforce roll\-outs and company best practices.
* Conduct regular site visits to ensure that the property has proper curb appeal, appropriate signage, models are clean and set up accurately, and all marketing opportunities are leveraged.
* Regularly review and understand the market landscape to stay abreast of competitor strengths, weaknesses, and marketing initiatives.
* Continually research and make recommendations on new opportunities and marketing channels in the local market.
* Attend company meetings and industry education conferences.


Qualifications:
* Multifamily marketing experience preferred.
* BA / BS degree strongly preferred.
* Must have previous experience with property management software.
* Graphic design experience is a plus.
* Excellent written, verbal and customer service skills. Ability to persuasively express ideas and opinions to individuals and groups.
* Expert proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint.
* Ability and willingness to travel.
* Must be willing to work flexible hours/days/weekends when needed.
* Analytical skills with previous experience developing strategic marketing plans.
* Proven track record of achieving marketing goals and demonstrating results from previous positions.
* Proven track record in establishing and maintaining strong relationships with management team.
* Strong leadership and interpersonal skills, including the ability to work with senior executives and in teams.

 **Benefits**  

Willow Bridge Property Company offers competitive pay, a benefits package that includes health, dental, vision and 401(k) and opportunities for career development and advancement.

title: Integrated Marketing Manager
description:
**INTEGRATED MARKETING MANAGER**  

We’re a major player in the automotive aftermarket accessory space with a start\-up mindset — and we’re looking for an Integrated Marketing Manager to match. This is a role for someone who thrives on variety, with the chance to touch every facet of marketing — from creative development to media planning and full\-scale campaign execution. It’s an opportunity to make a tangible impact in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment. If you’re excited by seeing your work out in the world and influencing key outcomes across both DTC and B2B channels, you’ll fit right in. ***Please note that this is an onsite role at our corporate office in Bolingbrook, IL.*** **As an Integrated Marketing Manager, you will** **Strategic Marketing \& Planning*** Partner with key stakeholders to develop integrated marketing programs that drive measurable business outcomes
* Support go\-to\-market planning for new product launches, including positioning, messaging, channel strategy and implementation
* Research, monitor and analyze industry trends and competitor products and marketing activities
* Contribute to account planning, forecasting and budget management

 **Brand Management, Advocacy and Engagement*** Serve as a steward of the brand, ensuring consistency in voice, tone and experience across all consumer touchpoints
* Support development and evolution of brand guidelines, visual identity systems and messaging frameworks
* Collaborate with Creative/Studio teams to manage and maintain brand assets and asset management systems
* Identify and support opportunities in PR, brand advocacy and customer loyalty initiatives
* Review and assess incoming partnership inquiries from creators, agencies, and brands to identify high\-impact collaboration opportunities

 **Campaign Development \& Execution*** Collaborate with internal teams and external agencies on authoring of creative briefs and development of creative campaigns (evergreen, seasonal and product launches)
* Participate in media and creative planning sessions to ensure alignment across channels
* Lead execution of integrated campaigns across channels including OOH, print, social, web, email, SEM, video and B2B
* Partner with Digital, Web and Studio teams to deliver high\-quality, on\-strategy work

 **Project Management \& Cross\-Functional Leadership*** Oversee timelines, deliverables and communications across stakeholders to ensure projects stay on track
* Align cross\-functional teams and proactively identify risks, roadblocks and solutions
* Ensure cohesive execution across multiple workstreams and partners

 **How you work*** You bring a test\-and\-learn mindset, continuously optimizing based on performance and insights
* You are naturally curious and proactive, identifying opportunities and bringing forward thoughtful, data\-informed ideas
* You balance strategic thinking with hands\-on execution, able to zoom out while managing day\-to\-day details
* You take ownership and accountability, following through on commitments and delivering high\-quality outcomes
* You thrive in a fast\-paced, ever\-changing environment and adapt quickly to shifting priorities

 **Qualifications*** 8–10 years of experience in marketing or integrated marketing roles
* Strong strategic thinking with the ability to connect big\-picture ideas to execution
* Highly collaborative, with the ability to build relationships and influence cross\-functional stakeholders
* Excellent communication and storytelling skills
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
* Proactive, solutions\-oriented mindset with a bias toward action
* Creative thinker with a strong attention to detail
* Proficiency with project management and collaboration tools, including Asana, the Atlassian suite (Jira, Confluence) and Microsoft Outlook
* Working knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite: able to view, annotate and navigate creative files without needing a designer to hold your hand
* Experience with collaborative proofing platforms for managing review cycles, versioning, and stakeholder feedback

* Authorized to work in the United States
 **Why you’ll love working at WeatherTech**
WeatherTech is proud to offer employees a competitive salary and benefits package, opportunities for internal promotions and skill development, and clean and safe workspace. Employees are offered an array of perks including:* Outstanding BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois medical plans; as well as dental, vision, short\- and long\-term disability, and company\-paid life insurance
* 401(k) with employer matching
* Paid vacation, holidays, and personal time

title: Head of EMEA Field Marketing
description:
Marketing · Full\-time · London; Netherlands; Berlin

  

Our mission is to automate coding. The first step in our journey is to build the best tool for professional programmers, using a combination of inventive research, design, and engineering. Our organization is very flat, and our team is small and talent dense. We particularly like people who are truth\-seeking, passionate, and creative. We enjoy spirited debate, crazy ideas, and shipping code.


Our mission is to automate coding. The first step in our journey is to build the best tool for professional programmers, using a combination of inventive research, design, and engineering. Our organization is very flat, and our team is small and talent dense. We particularly like people who are truth\-seeking, passionate, and creative. We enjoy spirited debate, crazy ideas, and shipping code.

### **About the Role**


As the **Head of EMEA Field Marketing**, you are the chief marketing architect of our go\-to\-market engine across the Enterprise and Strategic teams. You will lead a high\-performing team of Field Marketing Managers to design, execute, and optimize regional marketing strategies that drive pipeline and revenue.


We are looking for a player\-coach who is as comfortable interpreting a dashboard as they are commanding a room during a QBR. If you think business\-first, are outcome\-obsessed and thrive in high\-growth, fast\-paced environments, we want to talk.

### **What You’ll Do**

### **Strategic Leadership \& Revenue Growth**

* **Architect the Theater Strategy:** Develop comprehensive marketing plans across regions, segments, and industries to both land and expand in the largest enterprise accounts.
* **Deliver theatre growth plans:** Build differentiated strategies, scalable programs, and world class events across buying personas and industries, delivering at\-scale and account\-based tactics. You also won’t be afraid to jump in and execute alongside your team!
* **Galvanize Sales Partnerships:** Act as a trusted strategic advisor to Sales and Inside Sales Leadership through compelling executive presentations, insights\-driven recommendations, and business reviews.
* **Outcome Optimization:** Track lead flow from initial touchpoint to closed\-won revenue. Partner with Sales Ops to refine conversion metrics and maximize ROI.

### **Team Orchestration \& Development**

* **Lead a Remote Fleet:** Manage and mentor a geographically dispersed team. You’ll be responsible for hiring, onboarding, and fostering a culture of high performance and continuous learning.
* **Drive cultural excellence:** Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, data\-driven decision\-making, and collaborative execution across geographically distributed teams.
* **Develop future leaders:** Own talent strategy including succession planning, career pathing, and leadership development to build bench strength for sustained growth.
* **Cross\-Functional Advocacy:** Serve as the "voice of the theater" back to Corporate Marketing to ensure regional needs are met and global initiatives are successfully localized.

### **Analytics, Insights \& Business Acumen**

* **Establish executive\-level reporting:** Build comprehensive dashboards and KPI frameworks that track pipeline, program ROI, and business impact.
* **Drive data\-informed strategy: Leverage analytics to identify growth opportunities, optimize resource allocation, measure program effectiveness, and demonstrate marketing's contribution to revenue.**
* **Continuously optimize:** Develop feedback loops and refinement processes that improve investment efficiency and program performance.
* **Budget Ownership:** Manage the EMEA roll\-up budget, ensuring every dollar is allocated toward high\-impact activities.

### **Your DNA**

* **Proven people leader:** 10\+ years of progressive leadership experience with a track record of building, scaling, and inspiring high\-performing marketing teams.
* **Coaching excellence**: Demonstrated ability to develop talent, drive career growth, and create environments where top performers thrive.
* **Change management prowess:** Ability to lead organizational transformation, drive adoption of new processes, and navigate ambiguity in fast\-paced, high\-growth environments.
* **The Data Storyteller:** You don't just report numbers; you provide insights. You are fluent in the metrics that drive the business.
* **The Communicator:** Exceptional presentation skills. You can build trust with C\-suite executives and external partners alike.

### **Technical \& Tactical Toolkit**

* **Mobility:** Ability to travel as needed to support theater events and team meetings.


\#LI\-DNI

title: Senior Manager, Event Marketing
description:
### **Job Description:**

**The Role**


Do you want to have an impact on our customers’ businesses and be part of a highly collaborative, cross\-functional team committed to improving the value of our event programs within Fidelity? We are looking for a Senior Event Marketing Manager with deep expertise across all phases of the event lifecycle, including strategic planning, implementation, and post\-event data analysis.


This senior event marketer will work alongside key marketing and business partners to lead innovative event strategies that focus on the marketing, promotion, and execution of high\-impact programs for our key clients and prospects. This role is responsible for integrating event initiatives with broader business and marketing objectives, delivering proprietary insights to the market, and ensuring alignment to brand and compliance standards. The result will be deeper client relationships, stronger engagement, and increased demand for our products and services.

**The Expertise and Skills You Bring**

* Progressive experience in an events marketing role; financial services experience a plus, but not required.
* You are a highly motivated, organized leader with the ability to manage and integrate multiple complex initiatives simultaneously.
* You bring strong critical thinking and problem\-solving skills, with comfort navigating ambiguity and making decisions with minimal oversight.
* You are passionate about process excellence, attention to detail, and continuous improvement.
* Collaboration is vital \- you cultivate trusted relationships and provide direction to partners throughout all stages of the event planning process.
* You are adept at scoping, prioritizing, and delivering against complex timelines while maintaining clear, proactive communication with diverse stakeholders.
* You demonstrate quick, clear\-headed decision\-making under pressure and thrive in a fast\-paced, demanding environment.
* You have experience influencing key partners (business leaders, compliance, internal marketing teams) and providing advisory input based on data and experience.
* A customer\-first perspective drives your work, from conceptualization through execution.
* Knowledge of project and event management tools including Salesforce, Workfront, and CVENT preferred.
* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field with significant, progressive experience in event marketing, program management, or a related discipline.

**Note: Fidelity will not provide immigration sponsorship for this position.**

**The Team**


This dynamic and collaborative event marketing team is part of Fidelity Institutional and Asset Management marketing organization, supporting institutional investors and financial intermediaries (banks, broker dealers, registered investment advisors). Our team is passionate about raising the voice of Fidelity in the marketplace by driving an event marketing strategy aligned to our business goals and objectives. We develop event experiences that will provoke conversation and help drive action, ultimately to help our clients succeed and grow. We partner with various groups across Fidelity to ensure we bring the best to our customers.


The base salary range for this position is $85,000\-171,000 USD per year.
Placement in the range will vary based on job responsibilities and scope, geographic location, candidate’s relevant experience, and other factors.


Base salary is only part of the total compensation package. Depending on the position and eligibility requirements, the offer package may also include bonus or other variable compensation.


We offer a wide range of benefits to meet your evolving needs and help you live your best life at work and at home. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage and emotional well\-being support, market\-leading retirement, generous paid time off and parental leave, charitable giving employee match program, and educational assistance including student loan repayment, tuition reimbursement, and learning resources to develop your career. Note, the application window closes when the position is filled or unposted.


Please be advised that Fidelity’s business is governed by the provisions of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, the Investment Company Act of 1940, ERISA, numerous state laws governing securities, investment and retirement\-related financial activities and the rules and regulations of numerous self\-regulatory organizations, including FINRA, among others. Those laws and regulations may restrict Fidelity from hiring and/or associating with individuals with certain Criminal Histories.


Most roles at Fidelity are Hybrid, requiring associates to work onsite every other week (all business days, M\-F) in a Fidelity office. This does not apply to Remote or fully Onsite roles. Please consult with your recruiter for the specific expectations for this position.
