title: Director, DCP and Partner Marketing Programs
description:
At Snowflake, we are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI\-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high\-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low\-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast\-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.


THE MISSION
Snowflake’s partner ecosystem stands as a **formidable growth engine**, uniquely positioned at the forefront of the data and AI revolution.


Today, thousands of world\-class partners are already thriving as they build, co\-sell, and innovate within the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. This vibrant community presents a **massive opportunity** to amplify our collective success. By evolving toward a unified, high\-octane marketing engine and sharpening our collaborative storytelling, we can more effectively showcase the distinct competitive advantages that make Snowflake the winning choice for enterprises worldwide.


As Director of Partner Marketing Programs, you will build the system that activates our global partner ecosystem at scale — while shaping the strategic narratives that position Snowflake and its partners as the default choice in the AI data economy.


You will operate at two levels simultaneously:

* **System builder:** architecting repeatable, global partner marketing programs
* **Storyteller:** crafting high\-impact joint narratives with our most strategic Data Cloud partners

  

WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS
The next phase of competition in data \+ AI will not be won by individual products — it will be won by ecosystems.


Snowflake is uniquely positioned with:

* A rapidly expanding **Marketplace**
* Deep partnerships across **ISVs, data providers, SIs, and hyperscalers**
* A growing set of **AI\-native workloads and applications**


But winning requires:

* Clear, differentiated **partner value propositions**
* Scalable **co\-marketing engines tied to revenue**
* Strong **product \+ partner narrative integration**


You will define how this comes together.

  

WHAT YOU’LL OWN  


1\. BUILD THE GLOBAL PARTNER MARKETING ENGINE* Own and evolve Snowflake’s partner marketing programs (including Partner Activate)
* Design a **scalable co\-marketing framework** across partner tiers with clear ROI expectations
* Define how partners engage across launches, campaigns, and lifecycle marketing
* Create repeatable motions that drive:
* Partner\-sourced pipeline
* Partner\-influenced revenue
* Marketplace engagement and transactions


2\. LEAD DATA CLOUD PRODUCT PARTNER MARKETING* Own co\-marketing strategy for Snowflake’s Data Cloud product ecosystem (ISVs \& data providers)
* Define **joint positioning and solution narratives** that clearly articulate how partners extend the AI Data Cloud
* Build and scale **Marketplace\-first marketing motions** to drive preference and consumption
* Partner with top\-tier ISVs (e.g., Dataiku, Hex, Blue Yonder) to create flagship joint GTM stories


3\. DEFINE PARTNER GTM FOR PRODUCT LAUNCHES* Build the partner motion for major product and platform launches
* Ensure partners are activated early with clear messaging, content, and campaigns
* Translate product innovation into **ecosystem\-driven growth**


4\. DRIVE CROSS\-FUNCTIONAL ALIGNMENT* Partner deeply with:
* Product Marketing
* Demand Generation
* Field Marketing
* Alliances \& Business Development
* Ensure partner narratives are embedded in **integrated campaigns**, not treated as an afterthought
* Align partner strategy with Snowflake Partner Network (SPN) tiers and revenue goals


5\. LEAD AND SCALE THE TEAM* Lead a team of Partner Marketing Managers
* Elevate the team from program execution to **strategic GTM leadership**
* Build a culture of accountability, measurement, and high\-impact storytelling


WHAT YOU BRING  


EXPERIENCE* 15\+ years in B2B technology marketing
* 5\+ years in partner marketing, alliances GTM, or ecosystem marketing
* Experience operating at scale in complex, multi\-product organizations


STRATEGIC STRENGTHS* **Ecosystem storytelling:** Ability to translate technical integrations into compelling joint value propositions
* **Program architecture:** Experience building scalable marketing systems tied to pipeline and revenue
* **Marketplace \& platform thinking:** Understanding of two\-sided ecosystems and partner incentives


ANALYTICAL RIGOR* Strong experience with attribution models across multi\-touch, multi\-partner sales cycles
* Proven ability to connect marketing programs to pipeline and revenue outcomes


LEADERSHIP* Track record of leading high\-performing teams
* Strong cross\-functional influence across Product, Sales, and Partnerships
* Executive presence with both internal stakeholders and external partners


OUTCOMES IN THE FIRST 18 MONTHS* A clear, scalable **global co\-marketing framework** is in place and adopted
* Snowflake Marketplace sees measurable growth driven by partner marketing
* Top strategic partners have **flagship joint narratives and campaigns in market**
* Internal teams consistently integrate partner messaging into core GTM motions


WHY JOIN SNOWFLAKE* Define how one of the world’s leading data platforms wins through its ecosystem
* Operate at the center of **AI, data, and applications convergence**
* Shape the go\-to\-market model for a category that is still being written
* Work with a high\-caliber team where partner marketing has a true seat at the table


THE OPPORTUNITY
This is not a traditional partner marketing role.


You are building the system and the story that will determine how Snowflake — and its ecosystem — wins the next era of data and AI.


If you are excited about operating at that level of impact, we should talk.


Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.


How do you want to make your impact?


For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com


The following represents the expected range of compensation for this role:

* The estimated base salary range for this role is $213,000 \- $279,300\.
* Additionally, this role is eligible to participate in Snowflake’s bonus and equity plan.


The successful candidate’s starting salary will be determined based on permissible, non\-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location. This role is also eligible for a competitive benefits package that includes: medical, dental, vision, life, and disability insurance; 401(k) retirement plan; flexible spending \& health savings account; at least 12 paid holidays; paid time off; parental leave; employee assistance program; and other company benefits.


To comply with pay transparency requirements and other statutes, you can notify us if you believe that a job posting is not compliant by completing this form.

title: Senior Event Marketing Manager
description:
Overview:
**Remote, NYC, Miami, Chicago 40% travel** **We Are Lucky Strike Entertainment.**
Bowling, Arcade, Family Entertainment Centers. One company. Twelve brands. A career built around unforgettable experiences. Hospitality at our core; we put people first by creating meaningful connections and delivering memorable experiences to every guest, every time.  

The **Senior Event Marketing Manager** will play a critical role in executing grand openings, rebrand launches, and national marketing events, while also acting as the primary liaison between the Event Sales and Marketing teams.  

This role owns the event process end to end, from planning through execution and post\-event reporting, while also supporting outbound sales efforts through the development of marketing materials, trade show coordination, and alignment on sales initiatives. The ideal candidate is both strategic and execution\-focused, with the ability to manage multiple stakeholders, timelines, and priorities. ***What You'll Do:***
Event Strategy, Planning \& Execution:* Lead the planning and execution of grand openings, rebrand launches, and key marketing events across markets
* Develop and manage event timelines, run\-of\-show documents, and execution plans
* Lead event planning meetings with marketing, operations, and center teams
* Own post\-event recaps, performance insights, and reporting to internal stakeholders

  

Sales \& Marketing Alignment:* Serve as the primary liaison between Event Sales and Marketing teams
* Partner with Event Sales to support outbound initiatives with marketing materials, messaging, and assets
* Coordinate the development of sales collateral, presentations, and event\-specific materials
* Ensure alignment between marketing campaigns and sales priorities to drive event bookings
* Support Event Sales presence at trade shows with branded materials, booth setup, and messaging alignment
* Identify opportunities to maximize lead generation and brand presence at industry events


Cross\-Functional Coordination:* Partner closely with marketing, operations, PR, and events teams to ensure consistent brand execution
* Act as the central point of contact for event\-related marketing requests from internal teams
* Coordinate with the Events department on marketing needs, timelines, and asset delivery

  

Operations \& Vendor Management:* Own vendor sourcing, communication, and execution (photographers, talent, media activations, etc.)
* Oversee shipment, inventory, and distribution of branded materials and event assets
* Manage on\-site execution as needed, ensuring brand standards and guest experience excellence
* Manage inbound marketing requests related to events, sales support, and in\-center activations

  

PR \& Partnership Integration:* Partner with PR and partnerships teams to integrate media, influencers, and brand partners into events
* Support execution of partnership campaigns, ensuring deliverables are met across events and activations
* Coordinate media visits, press moments, and on\-site coverage tied to events and openings
* Act as a key point of contact for partners, ensuring seamless execution and brand alignment

  

Budget \& Project Management:* Track event budgets, invoices, and overall spend
* Manage timelines and deliverables across multiple concurrent projects
* Support partnership execution and task management across campaign

 ***What You'll Bring**** 5\-7 years of experience in event coordination and marketing or partnership marketing.
* Experience working cross\-functionally with sales teams and supporting revenue\-driving initiatives.
* Strong organizational and time management skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Strong vendor management skills
* Ability to manage multiple events and priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
* Willingness to travel as needed to event locations.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office and/or Google Workspace; experience with project management tools (e.g., Monday) a plus.
* Passion for creating memorable guest experiences and representing an iconic entertainment brand.

***What*** ***You’ll*** ***Get***

* At Lucky Strike Entertainment, we’re committed to supporting your well being both on and off the job. Here’s a peek at some of the benefits available to our Corporate associates:
* Medical, Dental, Vision plans to select from based on your needs
* Wellness resources to help you along your wellness journey
* 401(k) Retirement Plans
* Employee Stock Purchase Program
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
* Vacation and Holidays
* Perks! Bowling, Boomer’s, and Waterpark discounts, Retail discounts, Event discounts and more!


Who We Are:

Lucky Strike Entertainment, formerly Bowlero Corp, is one of the world’s premier location\-based entertainment platforms. With over 360 locations across North America, Lucky Strike Entertainment provides experiential offerings in bowling, amusements, water parks, and family entertainment centers. The company also owns the Professional Bowlers Association, the major league of bowling and a growing media property that boasts millions of fans around the globe. For more information on Lucky Strike Entertainment, please visit ir.luckystrikeent.com.  

The pay range for this position is $110,000 \- $120,000  

Lucky Strike Entertainment offers performance\-based incentives and a competitive total rewards package which includes healthcare coverage and a broad range of other benefits. Incentives and/or benefit packages may vary depending on the position. More details regarding benefits are available here: www.bowlerocorp.com/careers/benefits

title: Events Marketing Specialist
description:
*Astound is a leading provider of internet, WiFi, mobile, and TV services, dedicated to connecting communities and empowering lives through innovative technology. We also keep businesses connected with dependable fiber infrastructure and internet solutions backed by award\-winning service, helping organizations thrive in an increasingly connected world.*

*At the forefront of digital transformation, we continuously evolve our offerings to meet the dynamic needs of our customers—delivering reliable connectivity and groundbreaking digital experiences.*

*Our commitment to excellence extends beyond infrastructure. We invest in our people through personalized training, coaching, and a supportive work environment that fosters growth and opportunity. Employees are empowered to represent a superior telecommunications company while making a meaningful impact in the communities we serve.*

*We offer a robust benefits package that includes rewards, recognition programs, and employee discounts—ensuring our team members are supported in both their professional and personal journeys. At Astound, we believe in creating astounding possibilities for everyone, everywhere.*

A Day in the Life of the Events Marketing Specialist:
The Event Specialist is responsible for the strategy and execution of major key sponsorships by planning and executing best\-in\-class events. This role leads the negotiation and execution of larger scale events with major for\-profit and non\-profit associations, managing all events with the intent of creating brand awareness and goodwill for Astound while effectively showcasing all Astound products and services.


* Negotiate, plan, and execute sponsorship and branding events within the Astound footprint
* Investigate, prepare documentation, and recommend new events and sponsorships
* Coordinate with market leaders regarding sales, community, and small/medium business events
* Respond to unsolicited event and sponsorship requests
* Procure and manage vendors and brand ambassadors
* Coordinate run\-of\-show for all planned events
* Organize and deliver event materials including signage, banners, and booths
* Assist with TV, radio, and digital promotional copy
* Coordinate reciprocal media opportunities
* Lead social outreach and promotional campaigns
* Track budgets, process invoices, and prepare reports
* Manage programmer relationships for new event opportunities
* Participate in meetings and cross‑channel marketing initiatives
* Travel up to 40% as required
* Weekend availability required
* Other duties as assigned

What You Bring to the Table:


Education:


* High School Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree preferred

Experience:


* 2–4 years of experience with large sponsorships or agency\-side campaigns

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:


* Strong organizational, interpersonal, and negotiation skills
* Proficiency in MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
* Experience with budget and expense management
* Cable, telecom, or mobile industry experience preferred

***We're Proud to Offer a Comprehensive Benefits Package Including:***

* 401k retirement plan, with employer match
* Insurance options including: medical, dental, vision, life and STD insurance
* Paid Time Off/Vacation: Starting at 80 hours per year, and increases based on tenure with the organization
* Floating Holiday: 40 hours per year
* Paid Holidays: 7 days per year
* Paid Sick Leave: Astound allows a number of paid sick hours per calendar year and varies based on state and/or local laws
* Tuition reimbursement program
* Employee discount program

\*Benefits listed above are for regular full\-time position


***Base Salary:*** *The base salary range in TX for this position is $55,000\- $83,000 annually, plus opportunities for bonus and benefits, if applicable. The base pay range represents the low and high end of the hiring range for this job. Actual pay will vary and may be above or below the range based on various factors including but not limited to relevant skills, experience, and capabilities. It is specific to TX and may not be applicable to other locations.*

title: Field Marketing Coordinator
description:
**Company Overview:**


Cellebrite’s (Nasdaq: CLBT) mission is to enable its global customers to protect and save lives by enhancing digital investigations and intelligence gathering to accelerate justice in communities around the world. Cellebrite’s AI\-powered Digital Investigation Platform enables customers to lawfully access, collect, analyze and share digital evidence in legally sanctioned investigations while preserving data privacy. Thousands of public safety organizations, intelligence agencies and businesses rely on Cellebrite’s digital forensic and investigative solutions—available via cloud, on\-premises and hybrid deployments—to close cases faster and safeguard communities.


To learn more, visit us at www.cellebrite.com, https://investors.cellebrite.com/investors and find us on social media @Cellebrite.  



  

**Position Overview:**


We are seeking a highly organized and execution\-focused Field Marketing Coordinator to support the planning, coordination, and execution of regional marketing programs and events. This role is critical to driving pipeline growth by ensuring seamless delivery of field marketing initiatives, including events, campaigns, and sales enablement activities.


The ideal candidate thrives in a fast\-paced environment, is detail\-oriented, and enjoys collaborating cross\-functionally with sales, marketing, and external partners.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

**﻿**

**Event \& Program Execution**

* Support the execution of field marketing programs including trade shows, conferences, webinars, and hosted events
* Coordinate logistics such as venue selection, vendor management, shipping, booth setup, and on\-site execution
* Manage event timelines, checklists, and deliverables to ensure flawless execution

**Campaign Coordination**

* Assist in the development and deployment of integrated regional marketing campaigns
* Coordinate with digital, content, and demand generation teams to align messaging and assets
* Support email campaigns, landing pages, and promotional activities tied to field initiatives

**Sales Alignment \& Support**

* Partner with regional sales teams to align marketing programs with pipeline goals
* Help manage lead flow, follow\-up processes, and post\-event engagement strategies
* Provide support for account\-based marketing (ABM) initiatives

**Operational \& Administrative Support**

* Track and manage event budgets, purchase orders, and invoices
* Maintain marketing calendars and program documentation
* Analyze and report on program performance, including attendance, engagement, and ROI

  

Requirements:
  

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or related field
* 1–3 years of experience in marketing, events, or program coordination
* Strong organizational and project management skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously with attention to detail

**Preferred:**

* Experience supporting B2B or technology marketing programs
* Familiarity with marketing automation and CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, Marketo, HubSpot)
* Experience with event platforms and tools (e.g., Cvent, Splash)

  

Office Location:
  

Remote

title: Integrated Marketing Manager | The Points Guy
description:
**This role requires a hybrid schedule and will be based in our New York, NY office (Tuesday through Thursday) and work fully remotely on Mondays and Fridays each week.**



The Points Guy (TPG) is the go\-to resource for smart, savvy travelers looking to get the most out of their points, miles, and travel experiences. We help millions of readers unlock more from every trip — and we're looking for an Integrated Marketing Manager to help us do the same with our brand partnerships.



As Integrated Marketing Manager, you will play a key role in developing and executing innovative marketing proposals that drive value for our clients. You will take ownership of cross\-functional collaboration to craft compelling program packages during pre\-sales and ensure a seamless handover for smooth execution in post\-sale.

  

**For The Points Guy website** **click here****. Curious how The Points Guy fits into Red Ventures?** **Click here****.**

  


**What You'll Do:**


* **Lead integrated marketing strategy across pre\-sale opportunities** by owning RFP responses, proactive pitches, and client presentations, translating business objectives into cohesive, multi\-platform programs across content, social, partnerships, and experiential.
* **Partner cross\-functionally to bring programs to life** by collaborating with Sales, Editorial, Product, Design, Social, PR, and Partnerships teams to ensure alignment from concept through execution.
* **Drive ideation and concept development** by leading brainstorms that produce insight\-driven marketing solutions, shaping ideas into compelling narratives that leverage TPG's authority and resonate with B2B and B2C audiences.
* **Translate audience insights into marketable programs** by leveraging data, category expertise, and TPG audience knowledge to develop strategic proposals, sponsorship packages, and scalable solutions that drive revenue.
* **Develop and package revenue\-generating opportunities** by structuring integrated programs across platforms and identifying new ways to monetize TPG's content, brand, and audience through both custom and repeatable offerings.
* **Contribute to go\-to\-market strategy and product innovation** through proactive pitches that highlight our value proposition across industries such as credit cards, hotels, airlines, cruises, and tourism organizations.
* **Own and evolve TPG's marketing materials and brand positioning** by maintaining and updating media kits, eblasts, client templates, and ETDs, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and a best\-in\-class representation of TPG in market.
* **Collaborate on pricing, packaging, and inventory strategy** by partnering with revenue and ad operations teams to align program structure with performance goals and inventory realities.

  


**What We're Looking For:**


* **5\+ years of experience** in a media organization or ad agency, with the ability to work independently and own RFP responses, pitches, presentations, and marketing collateral from concept through delivery.
* **A strong communicator and creative storyteller**, highly skilled in developing strategic, well\-crafted sales materials and pitch decks, and translating ideas into impactful, marketable sponsorship opportunities.
* **Highly creative and solutions\-oriented**, with the ability to generate innovative, insight\-driven ideas and adapt quickly in a fast\-paced environment.
* **Collaborative and adaptable**, comfortable working across teams and with diverse personalities, balancing internal priorities with client needs to drive aligned, effective outcomes.
* **Strong understanding of sales and marketing principles**, including how to position, package, and sell integrated programs and solutions.
* **Fluency in digital publishing metrics and analytics**, including yield, RPM, CPM pricing, audience packaging, and targeting.
* **Highly organized and detail\-oriented**, with excellent project management skills and the ability to manage multiple workstreams, prioritize effectively, and meet tight deadlines.
* **Data\-driven thinker**, with strong analytical skills and a track record of contributing to revenue growth and achieving business targets.

  


**Compensation:**


*This range reflects total cash compensation, which may include base salary only or base salary plus target bonus, depending on the role. Where eligible, equity may also be offered separately and not included below. Actual compensation varies based on location, experience, and qualifications.*


* New York City Total Cash Compensation Range: $112,000 \- $140,000 per year


Additionally, the following benefits are provided by Red Ventures, subject to eligibility requirements.


* Health Insurance Coverage (medical, dental, and vision)
* Life Insurance
* Short and Long\-Term Disability Insurance
* Flexible Spending Accounts
* Holiday Pay
* 401(k) with match
* Employee Assistance Program
* Paid Parental Bonding Benefit Program
* Flexible Paid Time Off (PTO): We believe time to rest and recharge is essential. That's why we offer a generous and flexible PTO policy. Full\-time employees accrue 20 days of PTO for a full calendar year annually, with an increase to 25 days after five years of service.

  


**Who We Are:**



Red Ventures is a global portfolio of high\-growth companies — spanning several U.S. businesses,a joint venture in the health services industry,and strategic investments in Europe. Their businesses include The Points Guy, Lonely Planet, Bankrate, the Allconnect Platform, RV Home Client Growth, RV Growth \& Transformation, Sage Home Loans Corporation, and more. Across the portfolio, Red Ventures businesses deliver seamless digital experiences for consumers, help Fortune 100 clients solve large\-scale digital growth challenges, and create world\-class experiences and opportunities for employees. Learn more at redventures.com and follow @RedVentures on LinkedIn and Instagram.



At Red Ventures, we believe diverse, inclusive teams are better. To help you better understand our core values and beliefs, we encourage you to watch this brief YouTube video: Our Belief Statements. This will give you insight into the principles that guide our work and our commitment to fostering an inclusive environment.



Red Ventures is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or any other basis protected by law. Employment at Red Ventures is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications.



We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to qualified individuals with disabilities. This includes providing reasonable accommodation where appropriate. Should you require a reasonable accommodation to apply or participate in the job application or interview process, please contact accommodation@redventures.com.

title: Director Integrated Marketing & Operations
description:
THIS IS A HYBRID POSITION REQUIRING IN\-OFFICE PRESENCE ON TUESDAY AND WEDNESDAY OF EACH WEEK. OFFICE IS LOCATED IN THE MAG MILE/STREETERVILLE AREA IF DOWNTOWN CHICAGO, IL.


**JOB SUMMARY:**

  

  

The Director, Integrated Marketing and Operations leads the Integrated Marketing Center of Excellence for the American Osteopathic Association and its innovation arm, AOIA. This role is responsible for advancing modern go to market strategy, product marketing, and campaign orchestration for the AOIA product portfolio, and the oversight of operations for the Center of Excellence and performance frameworks and integrates emerging marketing capabilities on behalf of the team.


Embedded within the Marketing and Communications in\-house agency team, the Director serves as a strategic partner to AOIA. The role ensures marketing strategies, campaign execution, and performance measurement are aligned with organizational priorities and delivers measurable outcomes.


The Director provides leadership and enablement for a network of integrated marketing professionals across the organization by establishing best practices, campaign frameworks, shared tools, and operational processes that elevate marketing effectiveness across all business lines.


**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:**


**Marketing Planning**


* Develop and implement GTM strategy and integrated marketing plans for AOIA products, programs and services, grounded in KPIs and organizational priorities.
* Collaborate with program owners and marketing and communications department colleagues to align messaging, timelines, performance benchmarks, and self\-service models across initiatives.


**Integrated Marketing Team Alignment**


* Oversee the integrated marketing strategy center of excellence comprised of peer\-level marketing and junior marketing specialists
	+ Ensure consistent performance, process workflows, analytics, and reporting of KPI’s for each business line
	+ Support and promote collaboration with the other marketing portfolios supporting other business lines, ensuring strategic needs are prioritized

**Collaborative Leadership**


* Collaborate with internal subject matter experts, IT, marketing, and communications peers responsible for corporate communications and reputation, and content and brand strategy to ensure alignment between integrated marketing strategies, brand narrative, and reputational communications.
* Oversee relationships with external agencies, consultants, and partners.


**Digital \& Email Marketing**


* Manage and execute email marketing campaigns using automation tools, segmentation, and performance optimization. Lead organic and paid digital marketing strategies across channels such as Google Ads, LinkedIn, and social platforms, ensuring alignment with program goals and ROI expectations.


**Content Strategy Development**


* Envision and manage compelling marketing content strategies and action plans tailored for various formats including web, social media, email, and collateral.
* Work with subject matter experts and stakeholders to ensure content accuracy, tone consistency, and brand alignment


**Website Management**


* Maintain and update AOIA’s existing website with fresh, relevant, and user\-focused content.
* Collaborate with external developers (as needed) to support user experience, SEO optimization, and accessibility standards and support internal users in a self\-service best practices model.


**A****nalytics \& Reporting**


* Track, analyze, and report on campaign and channel performance using tools like Google Analytics, social media insights, and email metrics.


**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE**


**Mandatory Experience:**

* Minimum of ten years of experience in integrated marketing, go to market product strategy, digital marketing, or marketing operations with a track record of leading multi\-channel campaigns and marketing initiatives.
* Demonstrated experience developing integrated marketing strategies that align business goals, audience engagement, and measurable performance outcomes.
* Proven ability to lead cross functional marketing initiatives within complex or matrixed organizations.
* Strong understanding of marketing operations including campaign management, marketing automation, CRM integration, and performance analytics.
* Experience developing audience segmentation and lifecycle marketing strategies across digital channels.
* Demonstrated proficiency with digital marketing and analytics tools including WordPress, Google Analytics, SEO and SEM platforms, CRM, and marketing automation systems such as Feathr, Pardot, HubSpot or similar technologies.
* Experience using design and content development tools such as Canva or Adobe Creative Suite.


**Special Skills:**


**Preferred**


* Experience in healthcare, product innovation, and consultative\-like environments]
* Experience working in a fast\-paced environment with a start\-up mentality.
* Working knowledge of Salesforce Marketing Cloud and/or HubSpot platform.
* Project management tools (i.e., Asana, Monday.com, Microsoft Project).


**Education:**


* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, or another related field.


**Licensure or Certification:**


* N/A


**WORKING ENVIRONMENT:**

**Physical:**


* Work is generally sedentary in nature, but requires occasional standing, walking, lifting, and moving objects (up to 25 pounds). Requires manual dexterity to use computers, telephones, and peripherals.
* Incumbents may occasionally work prolonged or irregular hours, including evenings and weekends.


**Mental**


* Work is performed in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment.
* Candidates are expected to be able to quickly adapt to stressful situations, exercise good judgement, communicate effectively orally and in writing, and interact appropriately with internal and external stakeholders.


**ENVIRONMENT:**


* Regular office environment
* This is a hybrid position requiring an in\-person presence in the office on Tuesday and Wednesday of each week.


Salary Range: $110,000\.00 \- $115,000\.00 Annually


The AOA offers the following benefits:


* Medical
* Dental
* Vision
* Life and AD\&D
* Long and Short\-term Disability
* FSA/HSA
* Commuter Benefits
* 401(k) with match


We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Director, Integrated Campaigns and Demand Generation
description:
**Description**


**Director, Demand Generation and Integrated Campaigns (2026\-46\)**


The Director of Demand Generation and Integrated Campaigns owns the strategy, planning, and performance of AAA\-ICDR’s marketing campaigns, and will drive activities that enable brand awareness, product growth, stakeholder adoption, and new category creation. This role ensures that all marketing campaigns are aligned to clear business goals, targeted to the right audiences, deliver measurable outcomes, and are executed as part of a cohesive demand strategy.  

This leader will partner closely with Business Development, Product, and other marketing functions to define campaign objectives, establish success metrics, and ensure that marketing efforts drive meaningful engagement and measurable business outcomes. The ideal candidate brings a strong command of B2B demand generation, campaign strategy, events, and performance optimization, and is comfortable bringing structure and accountability to a complex, cross\-functional environment.

**About Us**  

The American Arbitration Association (AAA), recognized among The NonProfit Times' 50 Best Nonprofits to Work For, offers a dynamic, fast\-paced environment where excellence meets opportunity. Our organization leads arbitration and mediation across diverse industries, providing professionals with unique exposure to multiple business sectors and conflict resolution practices. We foster growth through collaboration with experienced legal professionals and invest in our team through comprehensive professional development programs. At the AAA, we combine challenging work with meaningful recognition in an environment where merit drives success and learning never stops.

**Work Environment**  

Candidates must reside within 125 miles of NYHQ located in Downtown Manhattan, and offers a hybrid work arrangement.  

  

**Compensation**  

The salary range for this role is $125,000 \- $132,000\. This position is also eligible for an annual performance\-based incentive. Our competitive compensation package includes medical, dental, orthodontia, and vision coverage, a student loan repayment program, a 403(b) retirement plan with substantial company match, discounted pet insurance, and generous paid time off.

**Key Responsibilities**

 **Campaign Strategy \& Planning**

* Define and lead integrated campaign strategies that drive engagement, awareness, and leads across priority lines of business.
* Establish clear objectives, target audiences, messaging direction, and channel mix for all major campaigns, including events, product launches, and thought leadership.

**Event Strategy \& Business Alignment**

* Bring structure and consistency to event planning by following introducing clear frameworks for prioritization, tiering, and expected outcomes.
* Provide strategic guidance on how to maximize impact and engagement before, during, and after the event.

**Performance Ownership \& Optimization**

* Own campaign performance, including engagement, event attendance, and contribution to broader demand generation goals.
* Define KPIs and measurement frameworks for campaigns and events; regularly analyze results and identify opportunities for improvement.
* Drive continuous optimization of campaign strategies, messaging, and channel performance.

**Engagement and Nurture**

* Develop and lead always\-on nurture strategies that extend engagement beyond initial touchpoints, ensuring audiences are guided through a cohesive and intentional journey over time.
* Design segmented follow\-up approaches for key audiences, tailoring messaging and cadence to drive deeper engagement and progression.
* Build and optimize multi\-touch nurture programs that connect events, content, and campaigns — moving beyond one\-off communications to sustained audience engagement.

**Cross\-Functional Leadership**

* Collaborate closely with Product, Business Development, Content, Creative, and external partners to ensure alignment and effective execution.
* Serve as the central point of accountability for campaign direction, ensuring all stakeholders are aligned on goals, roles, and expectations.
* Provide clear campaign briefs and direction to execution teams.
* Conduct campaign post\-mortems to foster a culture of accountability and performance, and to drive continuous improvement within the marketing team.

 **Qualifications**

* 8–12\+ years of experience in B2B demand generation, integrated marketing, or campaign strategy roles in AI, legal tech, or professional services organizations. Experience running global marketing campaigns a plus.
* Hands\-on experience using Salesforce to track campaign performance, manage lead flows, and support alignment between marketing and business development.
* Proven success developing and leading multi\-channel marketing campaigns that drive measurable business outcomes.
* Strong experience leveraging events as a strategic marketing channel within broader campaign frameworks.
* Demonstrated ability to define KPIs, analyze performance, and optimize campaigns based on data.
* Experience working cross\-functionally with sales/BD, product, and creative teams.
* Strong leadership presence with the ability to influence stakeholders and bring structure to complex environments.
* Excellent strategic thinking, communication, and problem\-solving skills.

  

  

*The American Arbitration Association is an equal opportunity employer (EEO) and considers all employees and applicants for positions without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, marital status, or status as a covered veteran in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws.*

title: Field Marketing & Events Manager
description:
Remote USA
Growth – Growth /
Full\-time /
Remote

Immuta is the Data Provisioning Company, helping organizations provision secure, governed data access at the speed modern business demands. We automate access by policy and by request—eliminating tickets, reducing risk, and enabling both humans and AI systems to work with data safely and instantly.

Founded in 2015, Immuta is trusted by Fortune 500 companies and government agencies worldwide and operates as a hybrid workplace globally.

* Technology partners include Snowflake, Databricks, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Starburst.


* Immuta has been recognized by Forbes as a top American startup employer, by Inc. Magazine and BuiltIn as one of the best workplaces, and by Fast Company as one of the top 50 most innovative companies.


* $267 million in total funding. Lead investors include NightDragon, Snowflake, and Databricks, along with additional funding from ServiceNow, Citi Ventures, Dell Technologies Capital, DFJ Growth, IAG, Intel Capital, March Capital, Okta Ventures, StepStone, Ten Eleven Ventures, and Wipro Ventures.


* A hybrid workplace with offices in Boston, MA; Columbus, Ohio; College Park, Maryland.

  

### **ABOUT OUR TEAM**



We’re building a modern marketing organization at the forefront of Data and AI. Our focus is simple: create a smarter, more connected revenue engine that turns market attention into meaningful pipeline.



We're bringing together strong fundamentals with AI, automation, better data, and tighter alignment with sales. We’re investing in a model that’s more intelligent, more targeted, and built to drive real business outcomes.



This role sits within the Revenue Marketing team, where we translate strategy into pipeline. As Field Marketing \& Events Manager, you’ll own how we show up in\-market, creating high\-impact, targeted programs that drive engagement with priority accounts and convert that engagement into real opportunities.

  


### **YOUR ROLE**



As Field Marketing \& Events Manager, you’ll own the end\-to\-end field marketing calendar, from small, high\-touch executive programs to large\-scale industry events. You’ll operate as the connector between marketing and sales in\-region, ensuring every program is targeted, intentional, and tied to pipeline outcomes.



You’ll partner closely with Account Executives, Sales leadership, and Revenue Marketing to identify priority accounts, drive attendance, and ensure strong follow\-up. This role requires both creative program design and operational discipline, with a clear focus on measurable impact.



### **HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT:**

* **Own the Field Marketing Calendar:** Plan and execute a mix of local programs, roadshows, and regional events aligned to target accounts and sales priorities.
* **Deliver High\-Impact Events:** Lead execution of flagship events, sponsorships, and trade shows that generate meaningful engagement and pipeline.
* **Drive Account Engagement:** Partner with Sales to target the right accounts, secure attendance, and create high\-quality meetings and interactions.
* **Turn Events into Pipeline:** Ensure strong post\-event follow\-up, working with Account Executives to convert engagement into opportunities and accelerate active deals.
* **Measure What Matters:** Track program performance, pipeline influence, and ROI, continuously optimizing for efficiency and impact.
### **WHAT YOU'LL OWN:**


* Field marketing calendar, including local events, dinners, roundtables, and roadshows
* Execution of flagship events, sponsorships, and major industry trade shows
* Partner and co\-marketing events in collaboration with the Alliances team
* Target account selection and alignment with Sales for each program
* End\-to\-end event execution, including planning, logistics, and on\-site management
* Post\-event follow\-up processes in partnership with Account Executives and GTM Engineering
* Program measurement, including pipeline attribution and ROI analysis
* Budget management and cost optimization across field programs
### **WHO YOU ARE:**


* 5\+ years of experience in B2B field marketing, events, or demand generation within SaaS or enterprise technology
* Proven success executing events that drive pipeline and measurable business outcomes
* Strong experience partnering with Sales teams to drive account engagement and follow\-up
* Ability to manage both small, high\-touch programs and large\-scale events
* Experience with pipeline attribution, ROI measurement, and program optimization
* Highly organized with strong attention to detail and executional excellence
* Ability to operate independently while managing multiple programs and priorities
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
* Willingness to travel as needed to support events and field programs
$125,000 \- $150,000 a year
The On\-Target Earnings (OTE) range is $143,000 \- $165,000\.

*To provide greater transparency to candidates, we share base pay ranges for all U.S.\-based job postings. Our salary ranges are based on function, level, and geographic location, and are benchmarked for our company size and industry.*

*The final compensation package for this role will be determined during the interview process and is based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to, geographic location, internal equity, experience level, skill set, and training. The range shown above reflects the good\-faith hiring range for this role at the time of posting, consistent with applicable state and local pay\-transparency laws. Pay ranges may be adjusted in the future to reflect market changes.*

*This role may also be eligible for additional compensation, such as commission, variable pay, or equity, and comprehensive benefits, including medical, dental, vision, a 401(k) plan, and other applicable company programs.*
**Benefits**

At Immuta, our goal is to help bridge the gap between personal and professional growth, so that our team members can be well and thrive personally and professionally. After all, great professional success stories rarely happen without great personal success stories! Our generous benefits package given to all full time employees includes:

* 100% employer paid Healthcare (Medical, Dental, Vision) premiums for you and your dependents (including Domestic Partners)


* Stock Options


* Paid parental leave (Both Maternity and Paternity)


* Unlimited Paid time off (U.S. based positions)


* Learning and Development Resources



Immuta provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, marital status, age, disability (including disability due to pregnancy) or genetics, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Immuta complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment.

NOTICE TO THIRD PARTY RECRUITERS: Immuta does not accept candidates from third party recruiters. All candidates submitted through are considered to be submissions by the candidate and no submission will obligate Immuta to pay any third party for the referral or hiring of a candidate.
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. title: Marketing Campaign Manager
description:
***If you are currently on Young Life staff, please do not apply through this external jobs board. To apply for internal job opportunities, log in to Workday and use the*** ***Jobs Hub*** ***. Applying externally may delay your application and require you to resubmit internally.***

Because of Young Life’s exclusive Christian purposes of evangelism and discipleship as set forth in its Articles of Incorporation and in these Bylaws, and to reflect what has always been and will continue to be the position of Young Life, specifically the Christian belief that **each and every employee and volunteer of the corporation should minister as a servant of God with the primary responsibility of proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ and, as such, is an integral part of the Christian mission and ministry of the corporation** ,
  

Young Life shall only employ individuals or enlist volunteer leaders who: (a) profess a belief in Jesus Christ as their personal Savior and Lord; and (b) subscribe to the statements and policies required of all Young Life staff, including the Young Life Statement of Faith. Therefore, employees and volunteers of Young Life, during working and nonworking hours, shall: (i) be ready, willing and able to fulfill such ministry functions as may be required by the organization; (ii) refrain from conduct and statements that detract from the biblical standards taught and supported by Young Life, and (iii) abide by all policies and practices of Young Life including, without limitation, those related to religious belief or ministry activities.


At Young Life, our mission is to introduce adolescents to Jesus Christ and help them grow in their faith. We invite you to join us in this life\-changing work, building relationships and creating spaces where young people feel seen, known, and loved.


**Essential Functions:**
------------------------

* Strategy \& Planning: Lead the creation and optimization of multi\-channel marketing strategies to drive engagement, revenue, or brand awareness.
* Project Management: Independently manage project timelines and deliverables from concept to launch, often handling multiple tasks simultaneously.
* Data Analysis \& Reporting: Experience with digital marketing platforms and metrics to track performance, analyze behavior, and provide actionable insights for optimization.
* Content Management: Oversee and update content across various digital communication tools, ensuring adherence to style guides and communication standards.
* Stakeholder Communication: Act as a central point of contact, communicating campaign objectives and results to stakeholders to facilitate data\-driven decisions.

**Qualifications:**
-------------------

* **Education** : Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, PR, or a related field.
* **Technical Skills** : Proficiency in enterprise communication systems and digital communication tools.
* **Experience** : Minimum of 3 years of relevant marketing experience (agency or multi\-functional environments preferred).
* **Communication** : Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a strong attention to detail in editing and proofreading.
* **Mission Alignment** : A commitment to the organization's mission, including affirmation of Young Life’s Statement of Faith.
* **Travel:** Minimal travel required for National and Team meetings

**Job Specific Working Conditions:**

### **Key Responsibilities**

* **Strategy and Consultation**  

Serves as a thought partner for campaign directors across the mission of Young Life around the world in audience segmentation, offer creation and alignment, distribution strategies, reporting, and project management and coordination.
* **Copywriting \& Messaging:**  

Write compelling, mission\-aligned content for a variety of audiences and formats, including email, social, direct response appeals, donor reports, and landing pages. Create messaging to combat donor attrition and improve retention.
* **Creative Design:**  

Develop on\-brand visual assets using **Canva** (or other tools) for digital and print distribution. Collaborate with internal designers and writers when needed.
* **Email Marketing:**  

Plan, write, design, and manage donor email journeys using platforms like **Emma** and **Salesforce Marketing Cloud** .
* **Website Content:**  

Oversee content and light design updates on key donor\-facing webpages using platforms such as **TimeSites** or open CMS systems.
* **Project Management:**  

Lead and execute projects independently, coordinating with development, creative, and field teams. Own timelines and deliverables from concept to launch.
* **Performance Reporting \& Optimization:**  

Leverage **Salesforce reports** , Google Analytics, Meta Ads reporting, and other digital platforms to track campaign performance. Analyze donor behavior and marketing impact to provide actionable insights and recommend strategic optimizations to improve ROI and **donor retention** .
* **Digital Advertising:**  

Manage digital media buying and performance across **Google Ads, Meta Ads, and media partnerships** . Optimize campaigns for donor acquisition and retention.

### **Additional Qualifications**

* **5 years of relevant marketing experience (agency or multi\-functional environment preferred)**

title: Regional Marketing Manager - West Coast
description:
Description:
**Regional Marketing Manager – West Coast**

**Reports To:** Director of Community Marketing  

**Location:** This remote role offers flexibility for candidates residing in the West Coast, ideally Northern California.

 **Position Summary:**The Regional Marketing Manager will be responsible for executing strategic marketing initiatives determined by the COO/VP of Marketing and Public Relations across a portfolio of 20 to 35 communities across multiple markets within a defined region. This role is designed for a highly motivated individual with a proven track record in multifamily marketing, overseeing the implementation of comprehensive marketing campaigns, brand development, and resident engagement strategies. The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with the Regional Vice President, property management teams, corporate marketing, and external vendors to ensure that marketing efforts drive leasing performance, increase occupancy, and enhance brand presence for a company managing 30,000\+ units. The Regional Marketing Manager will be supported by a corporate marketing team for tasks related to implementations and help desk tickets.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

* Regional Strategy Development: Develop and manage the overall marketing strategy for a portfolio of communities within the assigned region, ensuring alignment with corporate goals and market\-specific needs.
* Brand Management: Oversee the implementation and consistency of the brand’s identity across all marketing channels, ensuring that all communities in the region reflect the company’s brand standards.
* Digital Marketing: Manage and optimize regional digital marketing efforts, including SEO, SEM, social media, email marketing, and website performance, to increase online visibility and lead generation.
* Leasing Support: Collaborate with on\-site teams to create and execute property\-level marketing plans tailored to each community’s unique characteristics and target demographics.
* Market Research and Analysis: Conduct regular market research to assess competitive positioning, emerging trends, and opportunities for growth. Leverage data to make informed marketing decisions and adjust strategies accordingly.
* Campaign Execution: Develop and manage regional advertising campaigns, including paid media (digital and traditional), email marketing, content development, and social media engagement to drive leasing and retention.
* Vendor Management: Oversee relationships with external vendors (digital agencies, graphic designers, photographers, etc.) to ensure high\-quality deliverables within budget and timelines.
* Budgeting and Reporting: Manage the regional marketing budget, ensuring that all campaigns are cost\-effective. Track and report on the performance of marketing initiatives using KPIs and ROI metrics.
* Event Planning: Lead regional event marketing strategies, including resident engagement events, leasing events, and community outreach to foster relationships and brand awareness.
* Training and Support: Provide marketing training and support to on\-site leasing and property management teams, ensuring consistent execution of marketing initiatives.
* New Development: Expertly guide marketing for new residential developments that may include retail, office, and hospitality components, collaborating on strategy, aligning branding and signage, coordinating promotions, and ensuring consistent brand experience across all channels.
* Business Development: Support business development efforts within the assigned region, as directed.


Requirements:

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field required.

**Experience:**

* Minimum of 5\+ years of marketing experience in multifamily real estate or a related field.
* Proven track record in managing large\-scale, regional marketing campaigns for multifamily portfolios (preferably over 10,000 units).

**Technical Skills:**

* Proficiency in digital marketing tools and platforms (Google Analytics, Google Ads, social media platforms, email marketing tools).
* Experience with property management software (e.g., Yardi, RealPage, etc.) and lead management systems.
* Strong knowledge of SEO/SEM, PPC, and website management.

**Leadership and Communication:**

* Strong leadership and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
* Ability to collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams, including operations, leasing, and executive leadership.
* Analytical Thinking: Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, generate insights, and make recommendations for continuous improvement.
* Creativity and Innovation: A creative thinker who stays ahead of industry trends and continuously looks for innovative ways to market communities and engage residents.

**Key Competencies:**

* Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Results\-driven and goal\-oriented, with a passion for delivering excellent customer service.
* Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to build strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
* Adaptability and problem\-solving skills to address evolving market trends and challenges.

**Working Conditions:**

* This position requires regional travel (approximately 40\-50%) to visit communities, attend events, and meet with on\-site teams.
* Flexible working schedule with occasional evening and weekend work required for events.

**Opportunities at Kairoi are Equally Seized**


Kairoi is an equal opportunity employer, and we strongly encourage applications from everyone regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status. We provide an accessible candidate experience, but please let us know if you need any additional accommodations or adjustments throughout the interview process. *Please note that employment with Kairoi is contingent upon successful completion of a background check and drug screening, as well as your possession of a valid driver’s license with no major infractions in the last 12 months. The multifamily industry is ever\-changing and there could be other duties required as needed and assigned.*

title: Regional Manager, Field Marketing – US Molecular Imaging - Cardiology - (North Central US)
description:
**Job Description Summary**
===========================


The Regional Field Marketing Manager will localize and activate brand strategy, enable regional sales teams, and execute educational and demand\-generation programs across their assigned region. This individual will serve as a bridge between national marketing and field execution, ensuring commercial excellence and agility in the rapidly evolving cardiology molecular imaging market. They will be a key player in creating clinical persuasion and referring physician demand in support of the imager sales teams.**Job Description**
===================

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Translate national marketing and brand strategies into executable regional/local plans that drive product adoption, by focusing on the clinical benefits and utility of cardiac PET. This role focuses on growing cardiac PET referrals, supporting site activations, and growing utilization within assigned region.
* Collaborate with regional sales directors and CPAMs to co\-develop regional and localized marketing in\-field demand generation activities and support the territory plans
* Identify, develop, and maintain a deep understanding of the Cardiology Network in territory referring mapping and patient flows.
* Execute and manage regional demand and clinical education marketing programs, including but not limited to referral development campaigns and cardiology in\-person and digital outreach, clinical cardiology grand rounds and other cardiology educational events/speaker presentations in collaboration with regional advocacy team.
* Support referrer site activations by delivering field materials, referral kits and training resources for cardiology referring physician offices.
* Plan and lead execution of regional/local demand generation initiatives including awareness campaigns, referral development programs, educational programing that address clinical understanding and key clinical barriers or enablers.
* Partner with sales leadership to develop territory specific go to market plans and customer activation activities ensuring aligned goals, messaging and resource allocation.
* Design and track KPIs for regional marketing execution including lead generation, campaign response, referring physician cardiac PET knowledge growth, and site conversion metrics.
* Collaborate with Marketing Franchise team and Centers of Excellence (Content, Digital, Events, MarCom) to develop and deploy tailored marketing assess and field kits (i.e. marketing materials for referrers to use) and programming based on regional/local customer and market insights.
* Integrate clinical “voice of field” into brand and GTM strategy through compliant and structured field intelligence reporting and active participation in brand and strategic planning, QBRs and launch team meetings
* Ensure Regulatory, Legal and Compliance stakeholder alignment for all field marketing activities including content, programs, local shows, clinical education meetings, peer\-to\-peer meetings and community engagements.
* Coordinate presence at key regional and local events in collaboration with events and franchise teams ensuring marketing strategies are being supported locally.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration:
* Refer customer questions and requests as appropriate to medical affairs, market access, and product development teams in line with company policies to maintain appropriate separation of duties.
* Will own regional level metrics for referrer activities, educations activities and demand generation goals.

**Key Capabilities:**

* Strong sales and marketing expertise in pharmaceutical diagnostics or MedTech supporting therapeutic enterprise\-scale program activation
* Deep knowledge of B2B2C healthcare models, preferably with experience in radiology, cardiology or relevant MedTech or diagnostic categories
* Strong sense for customer familiarity and a willingness to spend time in the field and translate voice of field and customer into strategic programming
* Strong sales coaching background with a focus on consistently high performance in a positive culture
* Ability to manage competing priorities for a complex team
* ROMS and ROI for marketing events, market activation and demand generation
* Strong analytical and communication skills with cardiology stakeholders
* Basic understanding of insurance coverage and reimbursement processes for pharmaceutical products

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in science, Social Sciences, Marketing, Business, or a related field; MBA or advance degree preferred.
* 10\+ years of pharmaceutical marketing experience, including product launch and brand management.
* Experience launching complex pharmaceutical diagnostics or MedTech, or capital/clinical programs in provider settings.
* Proven leadership experience in managing teams and cross\-functional collaboration.
* Strong strategic thinking, financial acumen, and excellent communication skills.
* Ability to manage complex projects with multiple stakeholders in a fast\-paced environment.
* Demonstrated ability to work in matrixed organizations with success increasing levels of responsibility.
* Willingness to travel 50% of the time in field and with customers and running regional/local events. This role will be responsible for covering the North Central US territory (including IL, OH, MN, WI, etc.)
* Candidate must currently reside in the Chicago or Cleveland area for consideration


We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas, now or in the future, for this job opening. For U.S. based positions only, the pay range for this position is $132,000\.00\-$204,000\.00 Annual. description:

Office**Job Description**


Reporting to the Senior Director of Marketing PSX, the Senior Manager, Commercial Marketing \& Global Events is responsible for leading integrated marketing strategy and the global event portfolio in support of PSX revenue growth objectives. title: Field Marketing Manager - Houston
description:
**What we need**



The **Field Marketing Manager** will bring Torchy's marketing strategies to life at the local level, focusing on understanding the unique needs of assigned market areas and tailoring marketing efforts to fit those needs. This role will develop localized marketing plans, train restaurant operators to effectively execute these plans, and provide hands\-on support in the field to ensure marketing initiatives are successful. The Field Marketing Manager will be a key player in enhancing our local presence, driving guest traffic, and ensuring brand consistency across all locations.


**What you'll do**


* **Market Area Analysis**: You will assess the unique needs, challenges, and opportunities within your assigned market areas, using insights to shape local marketing strategies.
* **Local Marketing Plan Development**: You will create targeted marketing plans that address the specific needs of your market, ensuring alignment with overall brand strategy and goals. These plans will include detailed action steps, visual elements, and clear objectives to be executed effectively.
* **Training and Support**: You will train restaurant operators and teams on executing marketing plans, ensuring they have the tools, resources, and knowledge to deliver effective campaigns.
* **Event Coordination**: You will manage and execute local events, festival participation, sponsorships, and promotions that help drive guest engagement and reinforce brand presence in the community. You will ensure these larger initiatives are effectively planned and executed, aligning with the needs of your assigned market.
* **Local Sponsorships:** You will evaluate and execute local sponsorship opportunities, ensuring they align with the brand’s goals and drive community engagement.
* **Boots on the Ground Support**: You will provide hands\-on support in the field, assisting with the execution of marketing activities and stepping in as needed to ensure plans are implemented smoothly. You will be actively present during key events, weekends, and non\-standard business hours to support local efforts.
* **Brand Consistency**: You will ensure all marketing activities reflect Torchy's brand identity, maintaining consistency across all locations and campaigns.
* **Collaboration**: You will work closely with the broader marketing team, HQ marketing for social initiatives, and other brand teams to align local efforts with national campaigns and share feedback from the field to inform overall strategy.
* **Performance Tracking**: You will measure the success of local marketing initiatives, tracking activities, promotions, redemptions, and sales trends. You will maintain an organized calendar of events and promotions, providing monthly reports to leadership and participating in quarterly business reviews to ensure we are achieving desired results.
* **Relationship Building**: You will build and maintain relationships with community partners, vendors, and local stakeholders to enhance Torchy's presence. You will also work on B2B initiatives, creating opportunities for collaboration that elevate the brand.
* **NSO (New Store Opening) Support**: You will be responsible for developing and executing New Store Opening (NSO) plans for your respective markets, ensuring all marketing initiatives are successfully implemented during launches.
* **Additional Duties**: You will perform other duties or special projects as assigned.


**How you'll do it**


* **Be Adaptable**: You will adjust plans and tactics as market needs shift, ensuring your approach remains relevant and effective in different environments.
* **Communication and Influence**: You will clearly articulate marketing plans, expectations, and feedback to stakeholders, including restaurant operators, community partners, and leadership. You will use your influence to gain buy\-in, motivate teams, and ensure alignment on all initiatives.
* **Build Relationships**: You will foster strong relationships with operators, community partners, and stakeholders to ensure effective collaboration and elevate Torchy's presence locally.
* **Be Proactive**: You will take initiative to identify opportunities for local marketing and step in where needed, including hands\-on support, to ensure successful execution of initiatives.
* **Organization**: You will manage multiple campaigns, events, and initiatives while maintaining an organized calendar and ensuring all activities align with broader brand goals and deadlines.


**What you need**


* **Minimum Qualifications**:
* + 5\+ years of experience in marketing, with a focus on field or local marketing.
	+ Proven experience developing and executing localized marketing plans.
	+ Strong project management skills with the ability to juggle multiple initiatives simultaneously.
	+ Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to train and motivate teams.
	+ Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with the ability to travel as needed.
* **Preferred Qualifications**:
* + Experience in the food \& beverage or hospitality industry.
	+ Familiarity with event planning and local sponsorships.

**Location:**


* **Houston, TX**
* **On\-site in our Houston locations (there will be times you will work from home)**

description:
**Why join Nextech?**We are a leader in specialty healthcare technology solutions.
We’re committed to hiring and retaining talent, which is why we invest in our employees through competitive pay, a generous bonus structure, great healthcare, a comprehensive wellness program, and many other benefits.
If you are a software engineer, finance or accounting professional, customer support specialist, or a business development expert with a passion for healthcare technology (just to name a few), we want to hear from you.
We are an equal opportunity employer with a commitment to diversity. All individuals, regardless of personal characteristics are encouraged to apply. If you are a candidate in need of assistance or an accommodation in the application process, please contact talent@nextech.com .  

  

**Job Summary**The Account\-Based Marketing (ABM) Manager is responsible for standing up and scaling a foundational ABM program focused on driving engagement, pipeline creation, and revenue progression within strategic target accounts. This role will define the ABM framework, operationalize target account processes across systems, and launch sales\-aligned, multi\-channel plays that advance buying groups through the funnel.  



The ABM Manager partners cross\-functionally—inside and outside the Marketing team—to align strategy, execution, and measurement for ABM success. This role is accountable for performance reporting, insights, and continuous optimization across target account segments and ABM plays.  



All activities must be in compliance with Equal Employment Opportunity laws, HIPAA, ERISA and other regulations, as appropriate.


### **Essential Functions**

* Define ABM strategy and program architecture (e.g., tiering, prioritization criteria, governance, engagement stages, and measurement), creating a repeatable framework that can scale across specialties and segments.
* Establish and maintain a target account management process in Salesforce (e.g., account identification/flagging, segmentation, buying group mapping support, and governance) and ensure target segments can be activated in marketing automation and ABM platforms.
* Create and run ABM operating rhythms (weekly/monthly cadences), intake/brief requirements, and cross\-functional workflows that enable consistent execution and fast iteration.
* Launch and optimize sales\-aligned, multi\-channel ABM plays (pilot refine scale) across target account lists/specialties to drive engagement, pipeline creation, and opportunity progression.
* Partner with Sales/BDR leadership to define target account objectives, buying group hypotheses, outreach orchestration, and SLAs; integrate marketing touches with BDR/sales sequences to improve conversion across stages.
* Develop account insights and reporting (engagement, coverage, pipeline, and revenue influence), and use test\-and\-learn experimentation to continuously improve ABM plays, messaging, and channel mix.
* Collaborate with Product Marketing, Content/Creative, Events, Sales Enablement, and Client Success to align account messaging, assets, and experiences to priority specialties and target accounts.
* Carry out additional responsibilities as assigned based on business need.
### **Minimum Requirements**

* + 5\+ years of B2B marketing experience with at least 3\+ years in account\-based marketing ABM supporting pipeline and revenue goals
	+ Demonstrated experience designing and executing sales\-aligned, multi\-channel programs for named accounts or defined account segments (e.g., email, paid media, web personalization, events/webinars, direct mail, and partner channels)
	+ Experience with ABM and intent platforms (e.g., Demandbase, 6sense, Terminus), web personalization, and/or account analytics/engagement reporting
	+ Strong working knowledge of Salesforce (or similar CRM) and marketing automation; ability to operationalize target account lists/segments, governance, and reporting across systems
	+ Experience using data and insights to develop a POV on program performance (sharing “what happened” and “what next”), including engagement and pipeline metrics
	+ Strong cross\-functional collaboration skills; ability to influence without direct authority and build durable relationships with Sales, BDRs, Marketing Ops, and Product Marketing
	+ Strong verbal and written communication skills to translate strategy, process, and performance insights for audiences across functions and levels
	+ Strategic thinker with strong program/project management skills and attention to detail
	+ Experience with account planning, buying group/role\-based messaging, and content strategy for complex B2B buying committees

Generous annual bonus opportunity
